60|            usage_api_key_id=usage_api_key_id,
61|            allow_private_hosts=allow_private,
62|        )
63|    except Exception:
64|        return None
65|    adapter = OmniRouteAvailabilityAdapter(transport)

{"content": "1|\"\"\"Bounded availability cache with documented TTL and stale-while-revalidate.
2|
3|The cache wraps an :class:`OmniRouteAvailabilityAdapter` (or any callable that
4|returns an :class:`AvailabilityReport`).  It deliberately never trusts a stale
5|entry for protected routing unless an explicit policy opt-in allows degraded
6|mode, and it isolates entries per provider/model/policy-version so one
7|source can never poison another.
8|
9|Design constraints (from issue #56 acceptance criteria):
10|- Cache keys include provider/model and the relevant policy/contract version.
11|- Expired or refresh-failed states are explicit ``unknown``/``error`` states.
12|- Concurrent refreshes are bounded and deduplicated.
13|- ``explain`` output identifies freshness and source.
14|\"\"\"
15|
16|from __future__ import annotations
17|
18|import threading
19|from collections.abc import Callable
20|from dataclasses import dataclass, field, replace
21|from datetime import datetime, timedelta
22|from typing import Any
23|
24|from llm_gate.availability import AvailabilityReport, _now
25|
26|
27|@dataclass(frozen=True)
28|class CacheKey:
29|    \"\"\"Identity for a cached availability observation.
30|
31|    Includes provider/model and the policy/contract version so a contract
32|    change invalidates stale entries instead of leaking them across versions.
33|    \"\"\"
34|
35|    provider: str
36|    model: str
37|    policy_version: str = \"policy-2026-07-13.1\"
38|
39|    @classmethod
40|    def for_candidate(cls, model_id: str, policy_version: str | None = None) -> CacheKey:
41|        provider = model_id.split(\"/\", 1)[0] if \"/\" in model_id else \"unknown\"
42|        return cls(
43|            provider=provider,
44|            model=model_id,
45|            policy_version=policy_version or cls.policy_version,
46|        )
47|
48|
49|@dataclass
50|class _Entry:
51|    \"\"\"Mutable cache entry holding the last good report and refresh bookkeeping.\"\"\"
52|
53|    report: AvailabilityReport
54|    stored_at: datetime
55|    ttl_seconds: int
56|    # Refresh coordination: only one in-flight refresh per key at a time.
57|    refreshing: bool = False
58|    refresh_error: str | None = None
59|    last_refresh_attempt: datetime | None = None
60|
61|    def age_seconds(self, now: datetime) -> float:
62|        return (now - self.stored_at).total_seconds()
63|
64|    def is_fresh(self, now: datetime, *, stale_window_seconds: int) -> bool:
65|        # Freshness is purely TTL-based; the stale window governs revalidation.
66|        return self.age_seconds(now) <= self.ttl_seconds
67|
68|    def is_within_stale_window(self, now: datetime, *, stale_window_seconds: int) -> bool:
69|        age = self.age_seconds(now)
70|        return self.ttl_seconds < age <= (self.ttl_seconds + stale_window_seconds)
71|
72|
73|def _default_clock() -> datetime:
74|    return _now()
75|
76|
77|def _confidence_for(report: AvailabilityReport) -> float:
78|    \"\"\"Deterministic, secret-free confidence in [0, 1] for an explain record.
79|
80|    Errors collapse confidence to zero. The cache/replay source (``\"cache\"``)
81|    is treated as low-trust. Otherwise confidence tracks the eligible share of
82|    observed candidates.
83|    \"\"\"
84|    if report.errors:
85|        return 0.0
86|    if not report.candidates:
87|        return 0.0 if report.source in (\"unknown\", \"cache\") else 0.5
88|    ratio = len(report.eligible) / len(report.candidates)
89|    if report.source in (\"unknown\", \"cache\"):
90|        return round(0.3 * ratio, 3)
91|    return round(ratio, 3)
92|
93|
94|@dataclass
95|class AvailabilityCache:
96|    \"\"\"Bounded, per-key availability cache with stale-while-revalidate.
97|
98|    ``get`` returns a cached report when fresh.  When the entry is within
99|    the stale window it returns the stale report (so callers keep working)
100|    but triggers a deduplicated refresh for next time.  When the entry is
101|    past the stale window (or absent) it performs a synchronous refresh and,
102|    on failure, returns an explicit ``unknown`` report rather than silently
103|    serving poisoned data.
104|    \"\"\"
105|
106|    source: Callable[[], AvailabilityReport]
107|    ttl_seconds: int = 60
108|    stale_window_seconds: int = 30
109|    policy_version: str = \"policy-2026-07-13.1\"
110|    clock: Callable[[], datetime] | None = None
111|    max_entries: int = 4096
112|    # Reentrant lock: ``get`` may call ``_refresh_locked`` while already holding
113|    # the lock (nested acquisition), so a plain ``Lock`` would deadlock.
114|    _entries: dict[CacheKey, _Entry] = field(default_factory=dict)
115|    _lock: threading.RLock = field(default_factory=threading.RLock)
116|
117|    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
118|        if not isinstance(self.ttl_seconds, int) or self.ttl_seconds <= 0:
119|            raise ValueError(\"ttl_seconds must be a positive integer\")
120|        if not isinstance(self.stale_window_seconds, int) or self.stale_window_seconds < 0:
121|            raise ValueError(\"stale_window_seconds must be a non-negative integer\")
122|        if not isinstance(self.max_entries, int) or self.max_entries < 1:
123|            raise ValueError(\"max_entries must be a positive integer\")
124|        # Reject a non-callable source early; the adapter is required.
125|        if not callable(self.source):
126|            raise TypeError(\"source must be callable and return an AvailabilityReport\")
127|
128|    def _key(self, model_id: str) -> CacheKey:
129|        return CacheKey.for_candidate(model_id, self.policy_version)
130|
131|    def get(self, model_id: str) -> AvailabilityReport:
132|        \"\"\"Return a cached-or-refreshed report for ``model_id``.
133|
134|        Fresh -> cached.  Stale-but-within-window -> cached + schedule refresh.
135|        Expired/missing -> refresh now; on refresh failure return explicit
136|        ``unknown`` rather than the stale entry.
137|        \
```

61|        import re
62|

77|        except Exception:

132|
133|        esc_reason: str = \"\"
134|        escalated = False
135|
136|        if eff_tier is not None and eff_tier < req_tier:
137|            req_tier = eff_tier
138|            esc_reason = heuristic_reason or \"\"
139|            escalated = True
140|
141|        safety_floor = req_tier if req_tier <= 1 else 3
142|        final_tier = min(task_tier, safety_floor, eff_tier if eff_tier is not None else 3)
143|
144|        candidates = []
145|        for name, cfg in self.providers.items():

147|

149|

152|                model=self.primary_model,
153|                provider=\"primary\",
154|                tier=0,
155|                reason=\"critical — never offload\"
156|                if final_tier == 0

## 🤖 Assistant (1784433020.2352955)

<details><summary>reasoning</summary>

Now I have the complete architectural picture. Let me design the #57 eligibility gate.

Current flow:
- `IntelligenceService.route()` builds `candidates` from `fetch_models(name, cfg, ttl)` for each provider (catalog discovery state).
- Calls `select_best_model(candidates, final_tier, providers)` → returns `(best_model, _)`.
- `select_best_model` already filters `is_available and availability_state in {eligible, ready}` then ranks by tier/cost.

The #57 gap: `select_best_model` filters on **catalog discovery state** (`ModelInfo.availability_state`), which is set at catalog fetch time, NOT the **live `AvailabilityCache`** truth (which knows per-model `unknown`/`error`/`stale` from the actual OmniRoute runtime probe). So the gate needs to consult the live `AvailabilityCache` and exclude any model whose live availability is not eligible — before ranking.

Critical architecture decisions per the runbook/AC:
1. "Candidate filtering occurs before ranking in every route path." → The single seam is `select_best_model` (called only from `intelligence.route`). But also `dispatcher.py` has a decision path. Let me check — dispatcher uses `IntelligenceService.route` too (it's the same underlying). Actually `dispatcher.py` likely wraps the gate. Let me verify, but the cleanest approach: add the live-availability exclusion inside `select_best_model` itself (the single ranking seam), plus expose a pure eligibility function so both the router and the explain endpoint can reuse it. That satisfies "every route path" because all paths converge on `select_best_model`/`route`.

2. "No ranker, Ruflo plan, or RuVector result can reintroduce excluded candidates." → The exclusion must happen in `select_best_model` (the ranker) and the decision must not be overridable by Ruflo/RuVector (which are advisory hooks only — they already don't override, but the invariant test must prove it). I'll add an `eligibility.py` module with a pure `filter_eligible(candidates, cache, protected) -> (eligible, excluded)` and inject it at the top of `select_best_model`.

3. "Explain endpoint shows the complete pre-ranking eligible set and exclusions." → `/v1/route/explain` (no model_id) should return, from the live cache, the eligible set + exclusions across the cached model keys. I'll extend the no-`model_id` branch to include per-model exclusion state.

4. "Protected work fails closed when runtime truth is absent." → When `protected=True` (tier 0) the model is never offloaded anyway (it returns primary). So "runtime truth absent" = cache not configured OR a model's live report is `unknown`/`error`. For protected offload scenarios (if any), fail closed = exclude. The cache already returns explicit `unknown` on refresh failure, and `select_best_model` will treat `unknown`/`error`/`not eligible` as excluded. If cache is None (not configured) → no live truth: per "fail closed when runtime truth is absent", we should still filter on catalog state for non-protected, but for *protected* routes we must not select an unavailable model. Since protected routes use primary (never offload), that's already fail-closed. I'll make the gate: if cache present, exclude any model whose live report is not eligible; if cache absent, fall back to catalog `availability_state` filtering (current behavior) — which is the documented fail-open-but-safe baseline, NOT a regression. The "fail closed" invariant test will assert: with a cache returning `unknown` for a model, that model is never selected even if a Ruflo/RuVector-style override attempts to reintroduce it.

Let me check `dispatcher.py` to see if it has its own decision path that bypasses `select_best_model`. Earlier I read dispatcher.py but the output was empty. Let me re-read it. And read the rest of `models.py` RoutingDecision + ModelInfo + RuntimeCandidate.

Actually, I already have a good read on the architecture. Let me read dispatcher.py and the rest of models.py to be precise. Let me do that.
</details>

79|
80|def _as_candidate(value: RuntimeCandidate | dict[str, Any]) -> RuntimeCandidate:
81|    return value if isinstance(value, RuntimeCandidate) else RuntimeCandidate.from_dict(value)
82|
83|
84|def _as_snapshot(value: AvailabilitySnapshot | dict[str, Any]) -> AvailabilitySnapshot:
85|    if isinstance(value, AvailabilitySnapshot):
86|        return value
87|    candidates = [_as_candidate(item) for item in value.get(\"candidates\", [])]
88|    return AvailabilitySnapshot.from_dict({**value, \"candidates\": candidates})
89|
90|
91|def _timestamp(value: str | None) -> datetime | None:
92|    if not value:
93|        return None
94|    try:
95|        return datetime.fromisoformat(value.replace(\"Z\", \"+00:00\")).astimezone(timezone.utc)
96|    except ValueError:
97|        return None
98|
99|
100|def _signal(candidate: RuntimeCandidate, *names: str) -> Any:
101|    lowered = {name.lower() for name in names}
102|    for key, value in candidate.signals.items():
103|        if key.lower() in lowered:
104|            return value
105|        if isinstance(value, dict) and any(k.lower() in lowered for k in value):
106|            return value
107|    return None
108|
109|
110|def _number(candidate: RuntimeCandidate, *names: str) -> float | None:
111|    value = _signal(candidate, *names)
112|    if isinstance(value, dict):
113|        matching = {n.lower() for n in names}
114|        value = next((v for k, v in value.items() if k.lower() in matching), value)
115|        if isinstance(value, dict):
116|            value = value.get(\"value\", value.get(\"amount\"))
117|    try:
118|        return None if value is None or isinstance(value, bool) else float(value)
119|    except (TypeError, ValueError):
120|        return None
121|
122|
123|def _cost(candidate: RuntimeCandidate) -> float | None:
124|    return _number(candidate, \"cost\", \"cost_usd\", \"estimated_cost\", \"usd\")
125|

127|def _capabilities(candidate: RuntimeCandidate) -> frozenset[str]:
128|    return frozenset(item.lower() for item in candidate.capabilities)
129|

131|class SwarmDispatcher:
132|    \"\"\"Select the least-cost candidate using only a caller-supplied snapshot.\"\"\"
133|
134|    def __init__(self, policy: DispatchPolicy | None = None, *, clock: Any = None) -> None:
135|        self.policy = policy or DispatchPolicy()
136|        self.clock = clock
137|
138|    def dispatch(
139|        self,
140|        snapshot: AvailabilitySnapshot | dict[str, Any],
141|        *,
142|        policy: DispatchPolicy | None = None,
143|        dry_run: bool = True,
144|        now: datetime | None = None,
145|    ) -> DispatchResult:
146|        active = policy or self.policy
147|        snap = _as_snapshot(snapshot)
148|        current = now or (self.clock() if self.clock else datetime.now(timezone.utc))
149|        if current.tzinfo is None:
150|            current = current.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
151|        candidates = tuple(_as_candidate(item) for item in snap.candidates)
152|        explanations: list[AssignmentExplanation] = []
153|        global_reason = self._snapshot_reason(snap, current)
154|        eligible: list[RuntimeCandidate] = []
155|        for candidate in candidates:
156|            reasons = list(global_reason or ())

37|class OmniRouteHTTPTransport:
38|    \"\"\"Fetch a bounded allowlist of documented OmniRoute JSON operations.
39|
40|    Catalog and management credentials are deliberately separate. Response

47|        self,
48|        base_url: str,

50|        api_key: str | None = None,
51|        management_token: str | None = None,
52|        usage_api_key_id: str | None = None,
53|        runtime_sources: set[str] | frozenset[str] = _DEFAULT_RUNTIME_SOURCES,

58|        transport: httpx.BaseTransport | None = None,
59|        clock: Callable[[], datetime] | None = None,
60|    ) -> None:
61|        self.allow_private_hosts = {
62|            host.rstrip(\".\").lower() for host in (allow_private_hosts or set())
63|        }
64|        normalized = validate_upstream_url(base_url, allow_private_hosts=self.allow_private_hosts)
65|        parsed = urlsplit(normalized)
66|        host = (parsed.hostname or \"\").rstrip(\".\").lower()
67|        if host not in self.allow_private_hosts:
68|            raise ValueError(
69|                \"OmniRoute destination host must be explicitly present in the allowlist\"
70|            )
71|        if parsed.scheme == \"http\":
72|            try:

74|            except ValueError:

77|                ) from None
78|            if not address.is_loopback:

80|        path = parsed.path.rstrip(\"/\")

<br>

AI coding tools can end up re-reading large parts of your codebase on review tasks. `code-review-graph` fixes that. It builds a structural map of your code with [Tree-sitter](https://tree-sitter.github.io/tree-sitter/), tracks changes incrementally, and gives your AI assistant precise context via [MCP](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/) so it reads only what matters.

<p align=\"center\">
  <img src=\"diagrams/diagram1_before_vs_after.png\" alt=\"The Token Problem: 38x to 528x token reduction across 6 real repositories\" width=\"85%\" />
</p>

---

## Quick Start

```bash
pip install code-review-graph                     # or: pipx install code-review-graph
code-review-graph install          # auto-detects and configures all supported platforms
code-review-graph build            # parse your codebase
```

One command sets up everything. `install` detects which AI coding tools you have, writes the correct MCP configuration for each one, installs platform-native hooks/skills where supported, and injects graph-aware instructions into your platform rules. It auto-detects whether you installed via `uvx` or `pip`/`pipx` and generates the right config. Restart your editor/tool after installing.

<p align=\"center\">
  <img src=\"diagrams/diagram8_supported_platforms.png\" alt=\"One Install, Every Platform: auto-detects Codex, Claude Code, CodeBuddy Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Zed, Continue, OpenCode, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Qwen, Qoder, Kiro, and GitHub Copilot\" width=\"85%\" />
</p>", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}
```

### 🔄 Tool result (1784433295.5101898)
```
{"content": "1|# Code Review Graph — User Guide
2|
3|**Applies to:** v2.3.6
4|
5|## Installation
6|
7|```bash
8|pip install code-review-graph
9|code-review-graph install    # auto-detects and configures all supported platforms
10|code-review-graph build      # parse your codebase
11|```
12|
13|`install` detects which AI coding tools you have, writes the correct MCP configuration for each one, and installs platform-native hooks where supported. Restart your editor/tool after installing.
14|
15|To target a specific platform instead of auto-detecting all:
16|
17|```bash
18|code-review-graph install --platform codex
19|code-review-graph install --platform cursor
20|code-review-graph install --platform claude-code
21|code-review-graph install --platform codebuddy
22|```
23|
24|### Supported Platforms
25|
26|| Platform | Config file |
27||----------|-------------|
28|| **Codex** | `~/.codex/config.toml` + `~/.codex/hooks.json` |
29|| **Claude Code** | `.mcp.json` + `.claude/settings.json` |
30|| **CodeBuddy Code** | `.mcp.json` + `CODEBUDDY.md` + `.codebuddy/settings.json` + `.codebuddy/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` |
31|| **Cursor** | `.cursor/mcp.json` |
32|| **Windsurf** | `~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json` |
33|| **Zed** | `.zed/settings.json` |
34|| **Continue** | `.continue/config.json` |
35|| **OpenCode** | `opencode.jsonc` (preferred) or `opencode.json` |
36|| **Antigravity** | `~/.gemini/antigravity/mcp_config.json` |
37|| **Gemini CLI** | `.gemini/settings.json` |
38|| **Qwen Code** | `~/.qwen/settings.json` |
39|| **Kiro** | `.kiro/settings/mcp.json` |
40|| **Qoder** | `.qoder/mcp.json` |
41|| **GitHub Copilot** | `.vscode/mcp.json` |
42|| **GitHub Copilot CLI** | `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json` |
43|
44|The CodeBuddy project layout follows its official documentation for
45|[MCP configuration](https://www.codebuddy.ai/docs/cli/mcp),
46|[skills](https://www.codebuddy.ai/docs/cli/skills), and
47|[hooks](https://www.codebuddy.ai/docs/cli/hooks). The shared `.mcp.json` is
48|merged with JSONC awareness, while hook commands resolve the repository at
49|runtime so committed settings do not contain one developer's checkout path.
50|
51|## Core Workflow
52|
53|### 1. Build the graph (first time only)
54|```
55|/code-review-graph:build-graph
56|```
57|Parses your entire codebase. Takes ~10s for 500 files.
58|
59|### 2. Review changes (daily use)
60|```
61|/code-review-graph:review-delta
62|```
63|Reviews only files changed since last commit plus the graph-derived impact radius. Relevant review and impact responses include compact estimated `context_savings` metadata. Across the 6 benchmark repositories, graph queries use ~82x fewer tokens per question (median; range 38x–528x) than reading the whole corpus — see the [README benchmarks](../README.md#benchmarks) and [REPRODUCING.md](REPRODUCING.md) for the methodology.
64|
65|### 3. Review a PR
66|```
67|/code-review-graph:review-pr
68|```
69|Comprehensive structural review of a branch diff with blast-radius analysis.
70|
71|### 4. Watch mode (optional)
72|```bash
73|code-review-graph watch
74|```
75|Auto-updates the graph on every file save. Zero manual work.
76|
77|### 5. Visualize the graph (optional)
78|```bash
79|code-review-graph visualize
80|open .code-review-graph/graph.html
81|```
82|Interactive D3.js force-directed graph. Starts collapsed (File nodes only) — click a file to expand its children. Use the search bar to filter, and click legend edge types to toggle visibility.
83|
84|### 6. Semantic search (optional)
85|```bash
86|pip install \"code-review-graph[embeddings]\"
87|```
88|Then use `embed_graph_tool` to compute vectors. `semantic_search_nodes_tool` automatically uses vector similarity when matching embeddings are available and falls back to keyword/FTS search otherwise.
89|
90|Embedding providers are local sentence-transformers, OpenAI-compatible endpoints, Google Gemini, and MiniMax. Local embeddings use `CRG_EMBEDDING_MODEL`; OpenAI-compatible providers use `CRG_OPENAI_BASE_URL`, `CRG_OPENAI_API_KEY`, and `CRG_OPENAI_MODEL`. Cloud providers are opt-in and print an egress warning unless `CRG_ACCEPT_CLOUD_EMBEDDINGS=1` is set.
91|
92|Function/class documentation summaries are included in embedding text. For a
93|graph created by an older release, run a full build once before re-embedding so
94|all files gain that metadata. Embedding refresh after build/update/watch is
95|always default-off; opt in with an exact provider and model, for example:
96|
97|```bash
98|code-review-graph build \\
99|  --embedding-provider local \\
100|  --embedding-model all-MiniLM-L6-v2
101|```
102|
103|The same two options work with `update`, `postprocess`, and `watch`. They must be
104|provided together. A refresh only updates a previously embedded graph, refuses
105|to migrate vectors to a different provider/model/endpoint, purges deleted-node
106|vectors, and degrades provider or transport failures to graph-build warnings.
107|
108|### 7. Detect changes with risk scoring (v2)
109|```
110|Ask your MCP client: \"Review my recent changes with risk scoring\"
111|```
112|Uses `detect_changes_tool` to map diffs to affected functions, flows, communities, and test gaps.
113|
114|### 8. Explore architecture (v2)
115|```
116|Ask your MCP client: \"Show me the architecture of this project\"
117|```
118|Uses `get_architecture_overview_tool` for community-based architecture map with coupling warnings.
119|
120|### 9. Generate wiki (v2)
121|```bash
122|code-review-graph wiki
123|```
124|Creates markdown wiki pages for each detected community in `.code-review-graph/wiki/`.
125|
126|### 10. Multi-repo search (v2)
127|```bash
128|code-review-graph register /path/to/other/repo --alias mylib
129|```
130|Then use `cross_repo_search_tool` to search across all registered repositories.
131|
132|## Context Savings
133|
134|CRG reduces review context by sending graph-derived structural context instead of broad file dumps. The exact reduction depends on the repository and chang
```

### 🔄 Tool result (1784433295.5247111)
```
{"content": "1|# All Available Commands
2|
3|## Skills and Slash Commands
4|
5|These commands are installed for clients that support project skills or slash-command style workflows.
6|
7|### `/code-review-graph:build-graph`
8|Build or update the knowledge graph.
9|- First time: performs a full build
10|- Subsequent: incremental update (only changed files)
11|
12|### `/code-review-graph:review-delta`
13|Review only changes since last commit.
14|- Auto-detects changed files via git diff
15|- Computes blast radius (2-hop default)
16|- Generates structured review with guidance
17|
18|### `/code-review-graph:review-pr`
19|Review a PR or branch diff.
20|- Uses main/master as base
21|- Full impact analysis across all PR commits
22|- Structured output with risk assessment
23|
24|## MCP Tools
25|
26|### Core Tools
27|
28|#### `build_or_update_graph_tool`
29|```
30|full_rebuild: bool = False           # True for full re-parse
31|repo_root: str | None                # Auto-detected
32|base: str = \"HEAD~1\"                 # VCS diff base for incremental updates
33|postprocess: str = \"full\"            # \"full\", \"minimal\", or \"none\"
34|recurse_submodules: bool | None      # Falls back to CRG_RECURSE_SUBMODULES
35|```
36|

## 🤖 Assistant (1784433379.1873608)

Correct — I was conflating two things. Let me clarify the actual topology before I write any config:

- **Codex** (`~/.codex`) is your *local coding assistant UI* (the thing you drive interactively), not a background worker harness. Its Hindsight bank is just continuation memory.
- The **worker/swarm plane** for this portfolio is **ruflo** (which shells out to `ruflo`/`agentic-flow` → and those can call Codex-style workers via OmniRoute), with **OmniRoute** as the model/provider endpoint. That's what the runbook describes: "Codex workers through OmniRoute."
- I am **Hermes** (this CLI agent) — the orchestrator, not Codex.

So registering code-review-graph's MCP into `~/.codex/config.toml` was wrong-targeted: that only helps the interactive Codex TUI. For *my* orchestration + ruflo swarms, the relevant consumers are:
- **ruflo** (which loads its own MCP servers, and `npx ruflo init` writes `.claude/` + ruflo runtime config into the repo),
- and optionally **Hermes** itself (me) — but I don't consume MCP servers the way Claude Code does; I call tools/CLIs directly.

So the right move: point code-review-graph's MCP at the **ruflo/Claude-Code-style config that the swarm workers use**, not the interactive Codex TUI. But more importantly — do you even want the MCP wired into a coding-agent config, or do you just want `code-review-graph` available as a **CLI I invoke directly** during review/PR steps? The CLI (`detect-changes`, `review-pr`, `pre_merge_check`) works standalone without any MCP wiring, and that's what I'd actually call from my orchestration loop.

Let me step back and confirm the topology with you rather than guess, since I just mis-targeted a config write:

**My understanding of the stack you want:**
1. **Hermes (me)** — orchestrator, drives the atomic loop, calls tools/CLIs.
2. **ruflo** — swarm/agent orchestration layer; `npx ruflo init` in the repo wires its MCP + hooks so swarm workers (which may use Codex-under-OmniRoute) get proper tooling.
3. **ruvector** — optional advisory retrieval/semantic memory (not the deterministic authority).
4. **OmniRoute** — model/provider endpoint the workers route through (`:20128`, api key in `.zshrc`, **no management token** → availability `health` reads can't auth).
5. **code-review-graph** — review/blast-radius/risk tooling, invoked as a CLI by the review steps (and optionally as MCP for any Claude-Code-type worker).
6. **metaharness** — currently an **empty stub** on disk; not usable until populated.
7. **codex** — your interactive local assistant + Hindsight continuation memory bank (separate from the swarm).

Two questions so I wire things to the right target:

**Q1:** For code-review-graph — do you want it (a) available as a **CLI I call directly** during review/PR (no MCP wiring needed — already installed + graph built), or (b) also registered as an **MCP server into ruflo's/Claude-Code's config** for any agentic workers? I'd recommend (a) for now since the graph is built and the CLI is ready.

**Q2:** The **OmniRoute management token** is missing from `.zshrc`. The llm-gate availability adapter's `health` runtime source needs it. Options: (a) you provide `OMNIROUTE_MANAGEMENT_TOKEN`, (b) I wire the cache to **catalog-only** (no `health` runtime source) so it works with just the api key, or (c) leave availability `runtime` reads disabled. Which?

Once you confirm Q1/Q2, I'll finish ruflo init against the correct target and proceed to #57. I won't write any more config until you answer — I'd rather not mis-wire a second time.

Hindsight has a **Documents view** (uploaded source docs stored as memories). Let me query the codex bank (which has the most context) and also the hermes bank for any stored tool docs, and check what document-retrieval looks like via the API.

> 🔧 **tool_call**: `execute_code`
```json
{
 "code": "import json, urllib.request\n\ncfg = json.load(open(\"/home/nick/.hindsight/codex.json\"))\nbase = cfg[\"hindsightApiUrl\"].rstrip(\"/\")\ntoken = cfg[\"hindsightApiToken\"]\n\ndef recall(q, bank=\"codex\", top_k=15):\n    url = f\"{base}/v1/default/banks/{bank}/memories/recall\"\n    body = json.dumps({\"query\": q, \"top_k\": top_k}).encode()\n    req = urllib.request.Request(url, data=body, method=\"POST\", headers={\n        \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {token}\", \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"})\n    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=25) as r:\n        return json.loads(r.read())\n\nfor q in [\"OmniRoute documentation API endpoints health rate-limits auth\",\n          \"Hermes agent documentation configuration tools\",\n          \"ruflo ruflo swarm documentation\",\n          \"ruvector documentation usage\",\n          \"code-review-graph documentation\"]:\n    for bank in (\"codex\",):\n        try:\n            d = recall(q, bank)\n            res = d.get(\"results\", [])\n            print(f\"\\n### Q: {q}  (bank={bank}, n={len(res)})\")\n            seen=False\n            for m in res[:6]:\n                txt = m.get(\"text\") or m.get(\"content\") or \"\"\n                ftype = m.get(\"fact_type\") or m.get(\"type\") or \"\"\n                # only show if it looks like a doc/reference, not generic policy\n                if any(k in txt.lower() for k in [\"endpoint\",\"/api\",\"docs\",\"github.com\",\"readme\",\"usage\",\"serve\",\"mcp\",\"command\",\"install\"]):\n                    print(f\"  [{ftype}] {txt[:200]}\")\n                    seen=True\n            if not seen:\n                print(\"  (no doc-like memories; top hit:)\", (res[0].get('text') or '')[:120] if res else \"none\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"ERR {bank}: {e}\")\n"
}
```

11|  <a href=\"https://discord.gg/NousResearch\"><img src=\"https://img.shields.io/badge/Discord-5865F2?style=for-the-badge&logo=discord&logoColor=white\" alt=\"Discord\"></a>
12|  <a href=\"https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/blob/main/LICENSE\"><img src=\"https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green?style=for-the-badge\" alt=\"License: MIT\"></a>
13|  <a href=\"https://nousresearch.com\"><img src=\"https://img.shields.io/badge/Built%20by-Nous%20Research-blueviolet?style=for-the-badge\" alt=\"Built by Nous Research\"></a>
14|  <a href=\"README.zh-CN.md\"><img src=\"https://img.shields.io/badge/Lang-中文-red?style=for-the-badge\" alt=\"中文\"></a>
15|  <a href=\"README.ur-pk.md\"><img src=\"https://img.shields.io/badge/Lang-اردو-green?style=for-the-badge\" alt=\"اردو\"></a>

17|</p>
18|
19|**The self-improving AI agent built by [Nous Research](https://nousresearch.com).** It's the only agent with a built-in learning loop — it creates skills from experience, improves them during use, nudges itself to persist knowledge, searches its own past conversations, and builds a deepening model of who you are across sessions. Run it on a $5 VPS, a GPU cluster, or serverless infrastructure that costs nearly nothing when idle. It's not tied to your laptop — talk to it from Telegram while it works on a cloud VM.
20|
21|Use any model you want — [Nous Portal](https://portal.nousresearch.com), OpenRouter, OpenAI, your own endpoint, and [many others](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/integrations/providers). Switch with `hermes model` — no code changes, no lock-in.
22|
23|<table>
24|<tr><td><b>A real terminal interface</b></td><td>Full TUI with multiline editing, slash-command autocomplete, conversation history, interrupt-and-redirect, and streaming tool output.</td></tr>
25|<tr><td><b>Lives where you do</b></td><td>Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, and CLI — all from a single gateway process. Voice memo transcription, cross-platform conversation continuity.</td></tr>
26|<tr><td><b>A closed learning loop</b></td><td>Agent-curated memory with periodic nudges. Autonomous skill creation after complex tasks. Skills self-improve during use. FTS5 session search with LLM summarization for cross-session recall. <a href=\"https://github.com/plastic-labs/honcho\">Honcho</a> dialectic user modeling. Compatible with the <a href=\"https://agentskills.io\">agentskills.io</a> open standard.</td></tr>
27|<tr><td><b>Scheduled automations</b></td><td>Built-in cron scheduler with delivery to any platform. Daily reports, nightly backups, weekly audits — all in natural language, running unattended.</td></tr>
28|<tr><td><b>Delegates and parallelizes</b></td><td>Spawn isolated subagents for parallel workstreams. Write Python scripts that call tools via RPC, collapsing multi-step pipelines into zero-context-cost turns.</td></tr>
29|<tr><td><b>Runs anywhere, not just your laptop</b></td><td>Six terminal backends — local, Docker, SSH, Singularity, Modal, and Daytona. Daytona and Modal offer serverless persistence — your agent's environment hibernates when idle and wakes on demand, costing nearly nothing between sessions. Run it on a $5 VPS or a GPU cluster.</td></tr>
30|<tr><td><b>Research-ready</b></td><td>Batch trajectory generation, trajectory compression for training the next generation of tool-calling models.</td></tr>
31|</table>
32|
33|---
34|
35|## Quick Install
36|
37|### Linux, macOS, WSL2, Termux
38|
39|```bash
40|curl -fsSL https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash
41|```
42|
43|### Windows (native, PowerShell)
44|
45|> **Heads up:** Native Windows runs Hermes without WSL — CLI, gateway, TUI, and tools all work natively. If you'd rather use WSL2, the Linux/macOS one-liner above works there too. Found a bug? Please [file issues](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues).
46|
47|Run this in PowerShell:
48|
49|```powershell
50|iex (irm https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/install.ps1)
51|```
52|
53|The installer handles everything: uv, Python 3.11, Node.js, ripgrep, ffmpeg, **and a portable Git Bash** (MinGit, unpacked to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\hermes\\git` — no admin required, completely isolated from any system Git install). Hermes uses this bundled Git Bash to run shell commands.
54|
55|If you already have Git installed, the installer detects it and uses that instead. Otherwise a ~45MB MinGit download is all you need — it won't touch or interfere with any system Git.
56|
57|> **Android / Termux:** The tested manual path is documented in the [Termux guide](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/getting-started/termux). On Termux, Hermes installs a curated `.[termux]` extra because the full `.[all]` extra currently pulls Android-incompatible voice dependencies.
58|>
59|> **Windows:** Native Windows is fully supported — the PowerShell one-liner above installs everything. If you'd rather use WSL2, the Linux command works there too. Native Windows install lives under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\hermes`; WSL2 installs under `~/.hermes` as on Linux.
60|
61|", "total_lines": 264, "file_size": 17670, "truncated": true, "hint": "Use offset=61 to continue reading (showing 1-60 of 264 lines)", "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

<div align=\"center\">

<img src=\"./docs/screenshots/MainOmniRoute.png\" alt=\"OmniRoute Dashboard\" width=\"820\"/>

<br/>

# 🚀 OmniRoute — The Free AI Gateway

### Never stop coding. Connect every AI tool to **265 providers** — **90+ free** — through one endpoint.

**Plug Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Cline, Copilot & Antigravity into FREE Claude / GPT / Gemini. Auto-fallback.**
<br/>

**RTK + Caveman compression saves 15–95% tokens. Never hit limits.**

<br/>

**~1.6B documented free tokens/month** — up to **~2.1B in your first month** with signup credits — aggregated across the free tiers, plus a long tail of permanently-free, no-cap providers, and the compression above stretches every one further. ([how we count →](docs/reference/FREE_TIERS.md#tldr--how-much-free-inference-does-omniroute-actually-aggregate))

<br/>

46|## Chat Completions
47|
48|```bash
49|POST /v1/chat/completions
50|Authorization: Bearer ***
51|Content-Type: application/json
52|
53|{
54|  \"model\": \"cc/claude-opus-4-6\",
55|  \"messages\": [
56|    {\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"Write a function to...\"}
57|  ],
58|  \"stream\": true
59|}
60|```
61|
62|### Custom Headers
63|
64|| Header                   | Direction | Description                                                                                                                  |
65|| ------------------------ | --------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
66|| `X-OmniRoute-No-Cache`   | Request   | Set to `true` to bypass cache                                                                                                |
67|| `x-omniroute-no-memory`  | Request   | Set to `true` to skip memory + skills injection for this request (mirrors no-cache; avoids the per-call token/cost overhead) |
68|| `X-OmniRoute-Progress`   | Request   | Set to `true` for progress events                                                                                            |
69|| `X-Session-Id`           | Request   | Sticky session key for external session affinity                                                                             |
70|| `x_session_id`           | Request   | Underscore variant also accepted (direct HTTP)                                                                               |
71|| `Idempotency-Key`        | Request   | Dedup key (5s window)                                                                                                        |
72|| `X-Request-Id`           | Request   | Alternative dedup key                                                                                                        |
73|| `X-OmniRoute-Cache`      | Response  | `HIT` or `MISS` (non-streaming)                                                                                              |
74|| `X-OmniRoute-Idempotent` | Response  | `true` if deduplicated                                                                                                       |
75|| `X-OmniRoute-Progress`   | Response  | `enabled` if progress tracking on                                                                                            |
76|| `X-OmniRoute-Session-Id` | Response  | Effective session ID used by OmniRoute                                                                                       |
77|| `X-OmniRoute-Request-Id` | Response  | Request correlation id (when known)                                                                                          |
78|| `X-OmniRoute-Version`    | Response  | OmniRoute build version (alway
```

## 🤖 Assistant (1784433594.766835)

I now have the authoritative OmniRoute API contract. The critical findings for "which models are usable":

- `/v1/models` (Bearer api key) → catalog of all 3,628 models + combos. Descriptive only (no live health).
- **`/api/monitoring/health`** → provider health/circuit state. The doc's Monitoring section (offset 501) likely shows the response shape. 
- `/api/rate-limits`, `/api/resilience/model-cooldowns` → quota/lockout state.

520|

528|

530|| -------------------------- | ------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
531|| `/api/tunnels/cloudflared` | GET    | Read Cloudflare Quick Tunnel install/runtime status for the dashboard   |
532|| `/api/tunnels/cloudflared` | POST   | Enable or disable the Cloudflare Quick Tunnel (`action=enable/disable`) |
533|| `/api/tunnels/ngrok`       | GET    | Read ngrok Tunnel runtime status for the dashboard                      |
534|| `/api/tunnels/ngrok`       | POST   | Enable or disable the ngrok Tunnel (`action=enable/disable`)            |
535|

537|

540|| `/api/cli-tools/claude-settings`   | GET    | Claude CLI status   |
541|| `/api/cli-tools/codex-settings`    | GET    | Codex CLI status    |
542|| `/api/cli-tools/droid-settings`    | GET    | Droid CLI status    |
543|| `/api/cli-tools/openclaw-settings` | GET    | OpenClaw CLI status |
544|| `/api/cli-tools/runtime/[toolId]`  | GET    | Generic CLI runtime |

547|
548|### ACP Agents

553|| `/api/acp/agents` | POST   | Add custom agent or refresh detection cache              |
554|| `/api/acp/agents` | DELETE | Remove a custom agent by `id` query param                |
555|
556|GET response includes `agents[]` (id, name, binary, version, installed, protocol, isCustom) and `summary` (total, installed, notFound, builtIn, custom).
557|

559|

563|| `/api/resilience/reset`           | POST      | Reset provider circuit breakers                                                      |
564|| `/api/resilience/model-cooldowns` | GET       | List active per-(provider, connection, model) lockouts, sorted by remaining time     |
565|| `/api/resilience/model-cooldowns` | DELETE    | Clear a model lockout — body `{provider, model}` or `{all: true}` to wipe everything |
566|| `/api/rate-limits`                | GET       | Per-account rate limit status                                                        |
567|| `/api/rate-limit`                 | GET       | Global rate limit configuration                                                      |
568|
569|> All four `/api/resilience/*` routes require **management auth** (`requireManagementAuth`). See [Resilience (extended)](#resilience-extended) for a full breakdown of provider breaker vs connection cooldown vs model lockout.

```
{"content": "1|---
2|title: \"OmniRoute MCP Server Documentation\"
3|version: 3.8.40
4|lastUpdated: 2026-06-28
5|---
6|
7|# OmniRoute MCP Server Documentation
8|
9|> Model Context Protocol server with 104 tools across routing, cache, compression, memory, skills, proxy, pool, and context source operations.
10|>
11|> Source of truth: `open-sse/mcp-server/server.ts` computes **104 unique tools** with `countUniqueMcpTools()`: 42 canonical definitions (including the six CCR lifecycle tools and the agent-skills trio), plus memory (3), skills (4), GitHub skills (3), pool (6), gamification (8), plugins (8), Notion (6), Obsidian (22), and two RTK-only compression tools.
12|
13|## Installation
14|
15|OmniRoute MCP is built-in. Start it with:
16|
17|```bash
18|omniroute --mcp
19|```
20|
21|Or via the open-sse transport:
22|
23|```bash
24|# HTTP streamable transport (port 20130)
25|omniroute --dev  # MCP auto-starts on /mcp endpoint
26|```
27|
28|## Transports
29|
30|The MCP server exposes three transports, all backed by the same `createMcpServer()` factory:
31|
32|| Transport         | Where                                       | When to use                                          |
33|| :---------------- | :------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------- |
34|| `stdio`           | `open-sse/mcp-server/server.ts`             | IDE integrations (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.)      |
35|| `sse`             | `POST/GET /api/mcp/sse` via `httpTransport` | Browser/agent clients that need an event stream      |
36|| `streamable-http` | `POST/GET/DELETE /api/mcp/stream`           | Multi-session HTTP clients (`mcp-session-id` header) |
37|
38|The active HTTP transport (`sse` or `streamable-http`) is selected by the `mcpTransport` setting. Switching transports closes existing sessions on the other transport.
39|
40|### Remote access (manage-scope bypass)
41|
42|`/api/mcp/*` is in the LOCAL_ONLY tier (`src/server/authz/routeGuard.ts`) — by default only loopback hosts (`localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, `::1`) can reach it. Since v3.8.2, non-loopback clients may connect if they present an `Authorization: Bearer *** whose key carries the `manage` scope. This is the only way to reach the remote MCP server through a tunnel, reverse proxy, or public hostname.
43|
44|```bash
45|# Grant manage scope: open the dashboard API Keys page and toggle
46|# \"Management Access\" on the key, or POST scopes:[\"manage\"] when creating.
47|
48|# Then connect from a remote MCP client:
49|curl -i \\
50|  -H \"Host: your-public-host.example\" \\
51|  -H \"Authorization: Bearer ***\" \\
52|  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\
53|  -H \"Accept: application/json, text/event-stream\" \\
54|  -d '{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":1,\"method\":\"initialize\",\"params\":{\"protocolVersion\":\"2025-03-26\",\"capabilities\":{},\"clientInfo\":{\"name\":\"my-client\",\"version\":\"0\"}}}' \\
55|  https://your-public-host.example/api/mcp/stream
56|```
57|
58|A non-manage key (or no Bearer) returns `403 LOCAL_ONLY`. The sibling prefix `/api/cli-tools/runtime/*` is intentionally NOT bypassable — see [Route Guard Tiers — Manage-scope carve-out](../security/ROUTE_GUARD_TIERS.md#manage-scope-carve-out).
59|
60|## IDE Configuration
61|
62|See [MCP Client Configuration](../guides/SETUP_GUIDE.md#mcp-client-configuration) for Claude Desktop,
63|Cursor, Cline, and compatible MCP client setup.
64|
65|---
66|
67|## Essential Tools (8) — Phase 1
68|
69|| Tool                            | Scopes                | Description                                                   |
70|| :------------------------------ | :-------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------ |
71|| `omniroute_get_health`          | `read:health`         | Uptime, memory, circuit breakers, rate limits, cache stats    |
72|| `omniroute_list_combos`         | `read:combos`         | All configured combos with strategies (optional metrics)      |
73|| `omniroute_get_combo_metrics`   | `read:combos`         | Performance metrics for a specific combo                      |
74|| `omniroute_switch_combo`        | `write:combos`        | Activate or deactivate a combo                                |
75|| `omniroute_check_quota`         | `read:quota`          | Quota used/total, percent remaining, reset time, token health |
76|| `omniroute_route_request`       | `execute:completions` | Send a chat completion through OmniRoute routing              |
77|| `omniroute_cost_report`         | `read:usage`          | Cost report by period (session/day/week/month)                |
78|| `omniroute_list_models_catalog` | `read:models`         | Full model catalog with capabilities, status, pricing         |
79|
80|## Phase 1 — Search
81|
82|| Tool                   | Scopes           | Description                                                                                                                        |
83|| :--------------------- | :--------------- | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
84|| `omniroute_web_search` | `execute:search` | Web search through OmniRoute search gateway (Serper/Brave/Perplexity/Exa/Tavily/Google PSE/Linkup/SearchAPI/SearXNG) with failover |
85|
86|## Advanced Tools (11) — Phase 2
87|
88|| Tool                               | Scopes                               | Description                                                                               |
89|| :--------------------------------- | :----------------------------------- | :---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
90|| `omniroute_simulate_route`         | `read:health`, `read:combos`         | Dry-run routing simulation with fallback tree                                             |
91|| `omniroute_set_budget_guard`       | `write:budget`                       | Session budget with d
```

### 🔄 Tool result (1784433751.3443687)
```
{"content": "1|---
2|title: \"OmniRoute A2A Server Documentation\"
3|version: 3.8.40
4|lastUpdated: 2026-06-28
5|---
6|
7|# OmniRoute A2A Server Documentation
8|
9|> Agent-to-Agent Protocol v0.3 — OmniRoute as an intelligent routing agent
10|
11|The A2A surface has two faces:
12|
13|- **JSON-RPC 2.0** at `POST /a2a` (canonical entry point, defined in `src/app/a2a/route.ts`).
14|- **REST** under `/api/a2a/*` for dashboards and tooling (status, task list, cancel).
15|
16|Tasks are tracked by `A2ATaskManager` (`src/lib/a2a/taskManager.ts`, default 5-minute TTL). Skills are dispatched via `A2A_SKILL_HANDLERS` in `src/lib/a2a/taskExecution.ts`.
17|
18|## Agent Discovery
19|
20|```bash
21|curl http://localhost:20128/.well-known/agent.json
22|```
23|
24|Returns the Agent Card describing OmniRoute's capabilities, skills, and authentication requirements.
25|
26|The Agent Card's `version` field is sourced from `process.env.npm_package_version` (see `src/app/.well-known/agent.json/route.ts:13`), so it stays auto-synced with `package.json` on every release.
27|
28|---
29|
30|## Authentication
31|
32|All `/a2a` requests require an API key via the `Authorization` header:
33|
34|```
35|Authorization: Bearer YOUR_O..._KEY
36|```
37|
38|If no API key is configured on the server, authentication is bypassed.
39|
40|## Enablement
41|
42|A2A is controlled by the **Endpoints → A2A** toggle and is disabled by default. When disabled,
43|`GET /api/a2a/status` reports `status: \"disabled\"` and `online: false`; JSON-RPC calls to
44|`POST /a2a` return HTTP 503 with JSON-RPC error code `-32000`.
45|
46|---
47|
48|## JSON-RPC 2.0 Methods
49|
50|### `message/send` — Synchronous Execution
51|
52|Sends a message to a skill and waits for the complete response.
53|
54|```bash
55|curl -X POST http://localhost:20128/a2a \\
56|  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\
57|  -H \"Authorization: Bearer ***\" \\
58|  -d '{
59|    \"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\",
60|    \"id\": \"1\",
61|    \"method\": \"message/send\",
62|    \"params\": {
63|      \"skill\": \"smart-routing\",
64|      \"messages\": [{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"Write a hello world in Python\"}],
65|      \"metadata\": {\"model\": \"auto\", \"combo\": \"fast-coding\"}
66|    }
67|  }'
68|```
69|
70|**Response:**
71|
72|```json
73|{
74|  \"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\",
75|  \"id\": \"1\",
76|  \"result\": {
77|    \"task\": { \"id\": \"uuid\", \"state\": \"completed\" },
78|    \"artifacts\": [{ \"type\": \"text\", \"content\": \"...\" }],
79|    \"metadata\": {
80|      \"routing_explanation\": \"Selected claude-sonnet via provider \\\"anthropic\\\" (latency: 1200ms, cost: $0.003)\",
81|      \"cost_envelope\": { \"estimated\": 0.005, \"actual\": 0.003, \"currency\": \"USD\" },
82|      \"resilience_trace\": [
83|        { \"event\": \"primary_selected\", \"provider\": \"anthropic\", \"timestamp\": \"...\" }
84|      ],
85|      \"policy_verdict\": { \"allowed\": true, \"reason\": \"within budget and quota limits\" }
86|    }
87|  }
88|}
89|```
90|
91|### `message/stream` — SSE Streaming
92|
93|Same as `message/send` but returns Server-Sent Events for real-time streaming.
94|
95|```bash
96|curl -N -X POST http://localhost:20128/a2a \\
97|  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\
98|  -H \"Authorization: Bearer ***\" \\
99|  -d '{
100|    \"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\",
101|    \"id\": \"1\",
102|    \"method\": \"message/stream\",
103|    \"params\": {
104|      \"skill\": \"smart-routing\",
105|      \"messages\": [{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"Explain quantum computing\"}]
106|    }
107|  }'
108|```
109|
110|**SSE Events:**
111|
112|```
113|data: {\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"method\":\"message/stream\",\"params\":{\"task\":{\"id\":\"...\",\"state\":\"working\"},\"chunk\":{\"type\":\"text\",\"content\":\"...\"}}}
114|
115|: heartbeat 2026-03-03T17:00:00Z
116|
117|data: {\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"method\":\"message/stream\",\"params\":{\"task\":{\"id\":\"...\",\"state\":\"completed\"},\"metadata\":{...}}}
118|```
119|
120|### `tasks/get` — Query Task Status
121|
122|```bash
123|curl -X POST http://localhost:20128/a2a \\
124|  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\
125|  -H \"Authorization: Bearer ***\" \\
126|  -d '{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":\"2\",\"method\":\"tasks/get\",\"params\":{\"taskId\":\"TASK_UUID\"}}'
127|```
128|
129|### `tasks/cancel` — Cancel a Task
130|
131|```bash
132|curl -X POST http://localhost:20128/a2a \\
133|  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\
134|  -H \"Authorization: Bearer ***\" \\
135|  -d '{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":\"3\",\"method\":\"tasks/cancel\",\"params\":{\"taskId\":\"TASK_UUID\"}}'
136|```
137|
138|---
139|
140|## Available Skills
141|
142|OmniRoute exposes 6 A2A skills wired in `src/lib/a2a/taskExecution.ts::A2A_SKILL_HANDLERS`. Each skill module lives in `src/lib/a2a/skills/`.
143|
144|| Skill              | ID                   | Description                                                                                                     | Tags                       | Examples                               |
145|| :----------------- | :------------------- | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :------------------------- | :------------------------------------- |
146|| Smart Routing      | `smart-routing`      | Routes a prompt through the optimal provider/combo using OmniRoute's combo engine + scoring                     | routing, providers         | \"Route this prompt via the best model\" |
147|| Quota Management   | `quota-management`   | Reports per-provider quota state, helps callers decide when to throttle/switch                                  | quota, providers           | \"Check quota for anthropic\"            |
148|| Provider Discovery | `provider-discovery` | Lists installed providers with capabilities, free-tier flags, OAuth status                                      | providers, discovery       | \"What providers are available?\"        |
149|
```

## 🤖 Assistant (1784433771.1018813)

Now I have the complete, authoritative contracts for both endpoints — and critically, your point is validated: these are documented in the repo and I should have read them first. Key findings:

**`/api/mcp/sse` (MCP-Server.md):**
- OmniRoute exposes an **MCP server with 104 tools** (routing, cache, compression, memory, skills, proxy, etc.)
- The 403 I hit is because MCP is in the **LOCAL_ONLY tier** — needs a `Bearer` key with the **`manage` scope** (or loopback). My `OMNI_API_KEY` lacks `manage` scope.
- There's also a `streamable-http` transport at `/api/mcp/stream`.
- Auth is via **API key scopes** (`read:health`, `read:models`, etc.); wildcard `*` = full.
- Two essential tools for us: `omniroute_get_health` (uptime, memory, circuit breakers, rate limits) and `omniroute_list_models_catalog` (full catalog w/ capabilities+status+pricing).

**`/.well-known/agent.json` + `/a2a` (A2A-Server.md):**
- A2A is **disabled by default** (Endpoints → A2A toggle). The agent card is public but the JSON-RPC `/a2a` needs the API key.
- 6 A2A skills including `health-report` (aggregates circuit breaker/cooldown/lockout per provider) and `provider-discovery` — these are exactly the "which models are usable" answers.
- `message/send` with `skill: "smart-routing"` is how an agent routes through OmniRoute.

{"content": "1100|
1101|**Auth:** Requires management session (dashboard `auth_token` cookie) or a
1102|management-scoped API key.
1103|
1104|See [ACP Framework](../frameworks/ACP.md) for full details.
1105|
1106|---
1107|
1108|## Analytics & Observability
1109|
1110|Real-time analytics endpoints for monitoring routing, compression, and provider
1111|diversity. These power the `/dashboard/analytics/*` pages.
1112|
1113|### Auto-routing analytics
1114|
1115|| Method | Path                                 | Description                                                                                        |
1116|| ------ | ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
1117|| GET    | `/api/analytics/auto-routing`        | Aggregate auto-routing stats: total calls, strategy distribution, tier distribution, top providers |
1118|| GET    | `/api/analytics/auto-routing?days=7` | Time-windowed stats (default 24h)                                                                  |
1119|
1120|**Response example**:
1121|
1122|```json
1123|{
1124|  \"window\": \"24h\",
1125|  \"totalCalls\": 1234,
1126|  \"strategyBreakdown\": {
1127|    \"rules\": 800,
1128|    \"cost\": 200,
1129|    \"latency\": 150,
1130|    \"sla-aware\": 50,
1131|    \"lkgp\": 34
1132|  },
1133|  \"tierBreakdown\": {
1134|    \"ultra\": 100,
1135|    \"pro\": 500,
1136|    \"standard\": 400,
1137|    \"free\": 234
1138|  },
1139|  \"topProviders\": [
1140|    { \"provider\": \"openai\", \"calls\": 500, \"avgLatencyMs\": 850 },
1141|    { \"provider\": \"anthropic\", \"calls\": 300, \"avgLatencyMs\": 1200 }
1142|  ]
1143|}
1144|```
1145|
1146|### Compression analytics
1147|
1148|| Method | Path                         | Description                                                                           |
1149|| ------ | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
1150|| GET    | `/api/analytics/compression` | Aggregate compression stats: tokens saved, savings %, mode distribution, engine usage |
1151|
1152|**Response example**:
1153|
1154|```json
1155|{
1156|  \"window\": \"24h\",
1157|  \"totalOriginalTokens\": 5000000,
1158|  \"totalCompressedTokens\": 3500000,
1159|  \"totalSavings\": 1500000,

1161|  \"modeBreakdown\": {

1163|    \"standard\": 600,
1164|    \"aggressive\": 100,
1165|    \"ultra\": 50,
1166|    \"rtk\": 84
1167|  },
1168|  \"engineBreakdown\": {

1174|
1175|### Provider diversity tracking
1176|
1177|| Method | Path                       | Description                                                                                              |
1178|| ------ | -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
1179|| GET    | `/api/analytics/diversity` | Shannon entropy-based diversity tracking: prevents single points of failure by measuring provider spread |
1180|
1181|**Response example**:
1182|

1186|  \"shannonEntropy\": 2.45,
1187|  \"maxEntropy\": 3.17,
1188|  \"diversityRatio\": 0.77,
1189|  \"providerUsage\": {

1191|    \"anthropic\": 0.25,
1192|    \"google\": 0.2,
1193|    \"kiro\": 0.15
1194|  },
1195|  \"warnings\": [\"OpenAI accounts for 40% of traffic — consider diversifying\"]
1196|}
1197|```
1198|
1199|**Auth:** Requires management session or management-scoped API key.

1203|## Admin Operations

1225|| POST   | `/api/cli-tools/apply`                  | Apply a CLI tool configuration to a provider connection                                        |
1226|| GET    | `/api/cli-tools/backups`                | List CLI tool configuration backups                                                            |
1227|| POST   | `/api/cli-tools/backups`                | Create a backup of all CLI tool configurations                                                 |
1228|| POST   | `/api/cli-tools/[id]/restore`           | Restore a CLI tool from a backup                                                               |
1229|| GET    | `/api/cli-tools/antigravity-mitm`       | Antigravity MITM proxy status (the \"antigravity-mitm\" C

### 🔄 Tool result (1784433771.1875615)
```
{"content": "1|---
2|title: \"CLI Tools — OmniRoute\"
3|version: 3.8.40
4|lastUpdated: 2026-06-28
5|---
6|
7|# CLI Tools — OmniRoute
8|
9|Last updated: 2026-06-28
10|
11|OmniRoute integrates with three categories of CLI tools spread across three dedicated dashboard pages:
12|
13|| Page           | Route                   | Concept                                                                   | Count        |
14|| -------------- | ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------ |
15|| **CLI Code's** | `/dashboard/cli-code`   | Coding tools you point at OmniRoute (Client → CLI → OmniRoute → Provider) | 20           |
16|| **CLI Agents** | `/dashboard/cli-agents` | Autonomous agents you point at OmniRoute (same flow, broader scope)       | 6            |
17|| **ACP Agents** | `/dashboard/acp-agents` | CLIs that OmniRoute spawns as backend via stdio/ACP (reverse flow)        | see registry |
18|
19|Legacy routes redirect via 308: `/dashboard/cli-tools` → `/dashboard/cli-code`, `/dashboard/agents` → `/dashboard/acp-agents`.
20|
21|---
22|
23|## How It Works
24|
25|```
26|CLI Code's / CLI Agents (consumption flow):
27|Claude / Codex / OpenCode / Cline / KiloCode / Continue / Hermes Agent / Goose / ...
28|           │
29|           ▼  (all point to OmniRoute)
30|    http://YOUR_SERVER:20128/v1
31|           │
32|           ▼  (OmniRoute routes to the right provider)
33|    Anthropic / OpenAI / Gemini / DeepSeek / Groq / Mistral / ...
34|
35|ACP Agents (reverse spawn flow):
36|    Client request → OmniRoute → spawns CLI via stdio/ACP → response
37|```
38|
39|**Benefits:**
40|
41|- One API key to manage all tools
42|- Cost tracking across all CLIs in the dashboard
43|- Model switching without reconfiguring every tool
44|- Works locally and on remote servers (VPS, Docker, Akamai, Cloudflare Tunnel)
45|
46|---
47|
48|## Auto-configure with `setup-*`
49|
50|You do not have to write each tool's config by hand. OmniRoute ships a `setup-*`
51|command per supported CLI that reads the **live** model catalog from a running
52|OmniRoute (local or remote) and writes the tool's own config on your machine:
53|
54|```bash
55|omniroute setup-codex        omniroute setup-claude       omniroute setup-opencode
56|omniroute setup-cline        omniroute setup-kilo         omniroute setup-continue
57|omniroute setup-cursor       omniroute setup-roo          omniroute setup-crush
58|omniroute setup-goose        omniroute setup-qwen         omniroute setup-aider
59|```
60|
61|Each accepts `--remote <url> --api-key <key>` (configure a local tool against a
62|remote OmniRoute), `--dry-run` (preview without writing), and `--port`. Tools
63|without model auto-discovery (Cline, Kilo, Roo, Goose, Qwen, Aider, Gemini) take
64|`--model <id>` (and `--yes` for non-interactive runs). The launchers
65|`omniroute launch` (Claude Code) and `omniroute launch-codex` (Codex) spawn the CLI
66|with the right env injected and write no config at all.
67|
68|> **Full reference:** the master table — what each command writes, every flag,
69|> local vs remote, and which tools want a `/v1` suffix — lives in
70|> **[CLI Integrations](../guides/CLI-INTEGRATIONS.md)**.
71|
72|---
73|
74|## Source of Truth
75|
76|The unified catalog lives in `src/shared/constants/cliTools.ts` as `CLI_TOOLS: Record<string, CliCatalogEntry>`.
77|
78|Each entry has these fields (defined in `src/shared/schemas/cliCatalog.ts`):
79|
80|| Field                                           | Type                                                         | Description                                            |
81|| ----------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------ |
82|| `category`                                      | `\"code\" \\| \"agent\"`                                          | Which page the tool appears on                         |
83|| `vendor`                                        | `string`                                                     | Tool origin (\"Anthropic\", \"OSS (P. Gauthier)\")         |
84|| `acpSpawnable`                                  | `boolean`                                                    | Also usable as an ACP Agent (badge shown)              |
85|| `baseUrlSupport`                                | `\"full\" \\| \"partial\" \\| \"none\"`                              | Custom endpoint support level. `\"none\"` = MITM backlog |
86|| `configType`                                    | `\"env\" \\| \"custom\" \\| \"guide\" \\| \"custom-builder\" \\| \"mitm\"` | Configuration mechanism                                |
87|| `id`, `name`, `color`, `description`, `docsUrl` | standard                                                     | Core display fields                                    |
88|
89|Entries with `baseUrlSupport: \"none\"` are **not shown** in the dashboard pages — they are registered in the MITM backlog for plan 11 (see `_tasks/features-v3.8.6/refactorpages/_orchestration/_plan11-mitm-backlog.md`).
90|
91|---
92|
93|## 1. CLI Code's Catalog (20 tools)
94|
95|Tools that support custom base URL and appear in `/dashboard/cli-code`:
96|
97|| id | name | vendor | baseUrlSupport | configType | acpSpawnable |
98||----|------|--------|---------------|-----------|-------------|
99|| claude | Claude Code | Anthropic | full | env | true |
100|| codex | OpenAI Codex CLI | OpenAI | full | custom | true |
101|| cline | Cline | OSS (ex-Claude Dev) | full | custom | true |
102|| kilo | Kilo Code | Kilo-Org | full | custom | false |
103|| roo | Roo Code | Roo (OSS) | full | guide | false |
104|| continue | Continue | continue.dev | full | guide | false |
105|| qwen | Qwen Code | Alibaba | full | guide | true |
106|| aider | Aider | OSS (P. Gauthier) | full | guide | true |
107|| forge | ForgeCode | Antinomy HQ | full | custom | true |
108|| jcode | jcode |
```

<details><summary>reasoning</summary>

Now I have the complete architectural picture. Let me design the #57 eligibility gate.

Current flow:
- `IntelligenceService.route()` builds `candidates` from `fetch_models(name, cfg, ttl)` for each provider (catalog discovery state).
- Calls `select_best_model(candidates, final_tier, providers)` → returns `(best_model, _)`.
- `select_best_model` already filters `is_available and availability_state in {eligible, ready}` then ranks by tier/cost.

The #57 gap: `select_best_model` filters on **catalog discovery state** (`ModelInfo.availability_state`), which is set at catalog fetch time, NOT the **live `AvailabilityCache`** truth (which knows per-model `unknown`/`error`/`stale` from the actual OmniRoute runtime probe). So the gate needs to consult the live `AvailabilityCache` and exclude any model whose live availability is not eligible — before ranking.

Critical architecture decisions per the runbook/AC:
1. "Candidate filtering occurs before ranking in every route path." → The single seam is `select_best_model` (called only from `intelligence.route`). But also `dispatcher.py` has a decision path. Let me check — dispatcher uses `IntelligenceService.route` too (it's the same underlying). Actually `dispatcher.py` likely wraps the gate. Let me verify, but the cleanest approach: add the live-availability exclusion inside `select_best_model` itself (the single ranking seam), plus expose a pure eligibility function so both the router and the explain endpoint can reuse it. That satisfies "every route path" because all paths converge on `select_best_model`/`route`.

2. "No ranker, Ruflo plan, or RuVector result can reintroduce excluded candidates." → The exclusion must happen in `select_best_model` (the ranker) and the decision must not be overridable by Ruflo/RuVector (which are advisory hooks only — they already don't override, but the invariant test must prove it). I'll add an `eligibility.py` module with a pure `filter_eligible(candidates, cache, protected) -> (eligible, excluded)` and inject it at the top of `select_best_model`.

3. "Explain endpoint shows the complete pre-ranking eligible set and exclusions." → `/v1/route/explain` (no model_id) should return, from the live cache, the eligible set + exclusions across the cached model keys. I'll extend the no-`model_id` branch to include per-model exclusion state.

4. "Protected work fails closed when runtime truth is absent." → When `protected=True` (tier 0) the model is never offloaded anyway (it returns primary). So "runtime truth absent" = cache not configured OR a model's live report is `unknown`/`error`. For protected offload scenarios (if any), fail closed = exclude. The cache already returns explicit `unknown` on refresh failure, and `select_best_model` will treat `unknown`/`error`/`not eligible` as excluded. If cache is None (not configured) → no live truth: per "fail closed when runtime truth is absent", we should still filter on catalog state for non-protected, but for *protected* routes we must not select an unavailable model. Since protected routes use primary (never offload), that's already fail-closed. I'll make the gate: if cache present, exclude any model whose live report is not eligible; if cache absent, fall back to catalog `availability_state` filtering (current behavior) — which is the documented fail-open-but-safe baseline, NOT a regression. The "fail closed" invariant test will assert: with a cache returning `unknown` for a model, that model is never selected even if a Ruflo/RuVector-style override attempts to reintroduce it.

Let me check `dispatcher.py` to see if it has its own decision path that bypasses `select_best_model`. Earlier I read dispatcher.py but the output was empty. Let me re-read it. And read the rest of `models.py` RoutingDecision + ModelInfo + RuntimeCandidate.

So registering code-review-graph's MCP into `~/.codex/config.toml` was wrong-targeted: that only helps the interactive Codex TUI. For *my* orchestration + ruflo swarms, the relevant consumers are:

62|### Custom Headers
63|
64|| Header                   | Direction | Description                                                                                                                  |
65|| ------------------------ | --------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
66|| `X-OmniRoute-No-Cache`   | Request   | Set to `true` to bypass cache                                                                                                |
67|| `x-omniroute-no-memory`  | Request   | Set to `true` to skip memory + skills injection for this request (mirrors no-cache; avoids the per-call token/cost overhead) |
68|| `X-OmniRoute-Progress`   | Request   | Set to `true` for progress events                                                                                            |
69|| `X-Session-Id`           | Request   | Sticky session key for external session affinity                                                                             |
70|| `x_session_id`           | Request   | Underscore variant also accepted (direct HTTP)                                                                               |
71|| `Idempotency-Key`        | Request   | Dedup key (5s window)                                                                                                        |
72|| `X-Request-Id`           | Request   | Alternative dedup key                                                                                                        |
73|| `X-OmniRoute-Cache`      | Response  | `HIT` or `MISS` (non-streaming)                                                                                              |
74|| `X-OmniRoute-Idempotent` | Response  | `true` if deduplicated                                                                                                       |
75|| `X-OmniRoute-Progress`   | Response  | `enabled` if progress tracking on                                                                                            |
76|| `X-OmniRoute-Session-Id` | Response  | Effective session ID used by OmniRoute                                                                                       |
77|| `X-OmniRoute-Request-Id` | Response  | Request correlation id (when known)                                                                                          |
78|| `X-OmniRoute-Version`    | Response  | OmniRoute build version (alway
```

508|### Backup & Export/Import
509|
510|| Endpoint                    | Method | Description                             |
511|| --------------------------- | ------ | --------------------------------------- |
512|| `/api/db-backups`           | GET    | List available backups                  |
513|| `/api/db-backups`           | PUT    | Create a manual backup                  |
514|| `/api/db-backups`           | POST   | Restore from a specific backup          |
515|| `/api/db-backups/export`    | GET    | Download database as .sqlite file       |
516|| `/api/db-backups/import`    | POST   | Upload .sqlite file to replace database |
517|| `/api/db-backups/exportAll` | GET    | Download full backup as .tar.gz archive |
518|
519|### Cloud Sync
520|
521|| Endpoint               | Method  | Description           |
522|| ---------------------- | ------- | --------------------- |
523|| `/api/sync/cloud`      | Various | Cloud sync operations |
524|| `/api/sync/initialize` | POST    | Initialize sync       |
525|| `/api/cloud/*`         | Various | Cloud management      |
526|
527|### Tunnels
528|
529|| Endpoint                   | Method | Description                                                             |
530|| -------------------------- | ------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
531|| `/api/tunnels/cloudflared` | GET    | Read Cloudflare Quick Tunnel install/runtime status for the dashboard   |
532|| `/api/tunnels/cloudflared` | POST   | Enable or disable the Cloudflare Quick Tunnel (`action=enable/disable`) |
533|| `/api/tunnels/ngrok`       | GET    | Read ngrok Tunnel runtime status for the dashboard                      |
534|| `/api/tunnels/ngrok`       | POST   | Enable or disable the ngrok Tunnel (`action=enable/disable`)            |
535|
536|### CLI Tools
537|
538|| Endpoint                           | Method | Description         |
539|| ---------------------------------- | ------ | ------------------- |
540|| `/api/cli-tools/claude-settings`   | GET    | Claude CLI status   |
541|| `/api/cli-tools/codex-settings`    | GET    | Codex CLI status    |
542|| `/api/cli-tools/droid-settings`    | GET    | Droid CLI status    |
543|| `/api/cli-tools/openclaw-settings` | GET    | OpenClaw CLI status |
544|| `/api/cli-tools/runtime/[toolId]`  | GET    | Generic CLI runtime |
545|
546|CLI responses include: `installed`, `runnable`, `command`, `commandPath`, `runtimeMode`, `reason`.
547|
548|### ACP Agents
549|
550|| Endpoint          | Method | Description                                              |
551|| ----------------- | ------ | -------------------------------------------------------- |
552|| `/api/acp/agents` | GET    | List all detected agents (built-in + custom) with status |
553|| `/api/acp/agents` | POST   | Add custom agent or refresh detection cache              |
554|| `/api/acp/agents` | DELETE | Remove a custom agent by `id` query param                |
555|
556|GET response includes `agents[]` (id, name, binary, version, installed, protocol, isCustom) and `summary` (total, installed, notFound, builtIn, custom).
557|
558|### Resilience & Rate Limits
559|
560|| Endpoint                          | Method    | Description                                                                          |
561|| --------------------------------- | --------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
562|| `/api/resilience`                 | GET/PATCH | Get/update request queue, connection cooldown, provider breaker, and wait settings   |
563|| `/api/resilience/reset`           | POST      | Reset provider circuit breakers                                                      |
564|| `/api/resilience/model-cooldowns` | GET       | List active per-(provider, connection, model) lockouts, sorted by remaining time     |
565|| `/api/resilience/model-cooldowns` | DELETE    | Clear a model lockout — body `{provider, model}` or `{all: true}` to wipe everything |
566|| `/api/rate-limits`                | GET       | Per-account rate limit status                                                        |
567|| `/api/rate-limit`                 | GET       | Global rate limit configuration                                                      |
568|
569|> All four `/api/resilience/*` routes require **management auth** (`requireManagementAuth`). See [Resilience (extended)](#resilience-extended) for a full breakdown of provider breaker vs connection cooldown vs model lockout.
570|
571|### Evals
572|
573|| Endpoint     | Method   | Description                       |
574|| ------------ | -------- | --------------------------------- |
575|| `/api/evals` | GET/POST | List eval suites / run evaluation |
576|
577|### Policies
578|
579|| Endpoint        | Method          | Description             |
580|| --------------- | --------------- | ----------------------- |
581|| `/api/policies` | GET/POST/DELETE | Manage routing policies |
582|
583|### Compliance
584|
585|| Endpoint                    | 
```

I now have the complete OmniRoute monitoring/resilience contract. Critical for the "which models are usable" problem:

- `/api/monitoring/health` → `catalogCount`, `configuredCount`, `activeCount`, `monitoredCount` — a **provider-summary health check**. This is the key usability signal (which providers are *active*).
- `/api/resilience/model-cooldowns` → per-(provider, connection, model) lockouts — tells you exactly which models are currently locked out. **Requires management auth.**
- `/api/rate-limits` → per-account rate limit status. **Management auth.**

So the live usability picture requires **management auth** for the detailed per-model cooldown/health, but `/api/monitoring/health` (the summary) returned **200 with NO auth** earlier — so the *summary* health may be publicly readable, while detailed per-model lockouts need the token.
