```
79|
80|def _as_candidate(value: RuntimeCandidate | dict[str, Any]) -> RuntimeCandidate:
81|    return value if isinstance(value, RuntimeCandidate) else RuntimeCandidate.from_dict(value)
82|
83|
84|def _as_snapshot(value: AvailabilitySnapshot | dict[str, Any]) -> AvailabilitySnapshot:
85|    if isinstance(value, AvailabilitySnapshot):
86|        return value
87|    candidates = [_as_candidate(item) for item in value.get(\"candidates\", [])]
88|    return AvailabilitySnapshot.from_dict({**value, \"candidates\": candidates})
89|
90|
91|def _timestamp(value: str | None) -> datetime | None:
92|    if not value:
93|        return None
94|    try:
95|        return datetime.fromisoformat(value.replace(\"Z\", \"+00:00\")).astimezone(timezone.utc)
96|    except ValueError:
97|        return None
98|
99|
100|def _signal(candidate: RuntimeCandidate, *names: str) -> Any:
101|    lowered = {name.lower() for name in names}
102|    for key, value in candidate.signals.items():
103|        if key.lower() in lowered:
104|            return value
105|        if isinstance(value, dict) and any(k.lower() in lowered for k in value):
106|            return value
107|    return None
108|
109|
110|def _number(candidate: RuntimeCandidate, *names: str) -> float | None:
111|    value = _signal(candidate, *names)
112|    if isinstance(value, dict):
113|        matching = {n.lower() for n in names}
114|        value = next((v for k, v in value.items() if k.lower() in matching), value)
115|        if isinstance(value, dict):
116|            value = value.get(\"value\", value.get(\"amount\"))
117|    try:
118|        return None if value is None or isinstance(value, bool) else float(value)
119|    except (TypeError, ValueError):
120|        return None
121|
122|
123|def _cost(candidate: RuntimeCandidate) -> float | None:
124|    return _number(candidate, \"cost\", \"cost_usd\", \"estimated_cost\", \"usd\")
125|
126|
127|def _capabilities(candidate: RuntimeCandidate) -> frozenset[str]:
128|    return frozenset(item.lower() for item in candidate.capabilities)

131|class SwarmDispatcher:

149|        if current.tzinfo is None:

154|        eligible: list[RuntimeCandidate] = []
```

## 🤖 Assistant (1784436074.4168446)

I have the full picture now. The architecture already has a clean separation:

- `OmniRouteAvailabilityAdapter.evaluate()` → `AvailabilityReport(tuple[AvailabilityCandidate...], tuple[eligible...], ...)` — the normalized eligibility source.
- `ProbeRunner.run()` exists & tested, returns `ProbeObservation` → `as_runtime_observation()` → `RuntimeObservation`.
- `SwarmDispatcher.dispatch()` already filters `eligible` by `live_eligible`/availability and records per-candidate exclusion reasons.
- `AvailabilityCache` (from #56) wraps `Callable[[], AvailabilityReport]`.
- `intelligence.route()` and `router.select_best_model()` do NOT currently consult availability at all.

The cleanest, doc-compliant #57 wiring (and #72/#73):

1. Add a **probe-enriched adapter variant** that runs the catalog/runtime evaluate AND a bounded `ProbeRunner` (only when `availability_profile` is production), merging probe `RuntimeObservation`s so `ready`/`degraded`/`denied` truth is applied. Reuse `ProbeRunner`, no reinvention.
2. Make **`AvailabilityCache` the single source of truth** and have router/dispatcher/Gate/explain all consult it (so no ranker can reintroduce excluded candidates — the invariant).
3. Extend **`/v1/route/explain` (#73)** to surface the full candidate set, eligible set, per-candidate exclusion reasons, and cache confidence/refresh_error — from the same gate.
4. **Fail-closed for protected work** when runtime truth absent.

Let me read `gate.py` and `api.py` (explain endpoint) plus `availability_cache.py` so I wire to the exact existing signatures, then implement. This is the concrete next step.

```
18|from llm_gate.catalog import configured_catalog_filters, normalize_catalog
19|from llm_gate.gate import Gate
20|from llm_gate.intelligence import DEFAULT_PROFILE, DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS, IntelligenceService
21|from llm_gate.models import ProviderConfig
22|from llm_gate.omniroute import OmniRouteHTTPTransport
23|from llm_gate.proxy import BufferedUpstreamResponse, StreamedUpstreamResponse, UpstreamProxy
24|from llm_gate.security import bearer_matches, redact_text, validate_server_security
25|
26|# Singleton service instances
27|intelligence_instance: IntelligenceService | None = None
28|gate_instance: Gate | None = None
29|proxy_instance: UpstreamProxy | None = None
30|availability_cache_instance: AvailabilityCache | None = None
31|
32|DEFAULT_AVAILABILITY_TTL_SECONDS = 60
33|DEFAULT_AVAILABILITY_STALE_WINDOW_SECONDS = 30
34|
35|
36|def _build_availability_cache() -> AvailabilityCache | None:
37|    \"\"\"Build the bounded availability cache backed by the native OmniRoute transport.
38|
39|    Returns ``None`` when no OmniRoute endpoint is configured, so the server
40|    still boots without availability explainability.  The transport is
41|    loopback-only and credential-safe; a misconfigured base URL fails closed
42|    to ``None`` rather than crashing startup.
43|    \"\"\"
44|    base_url = os.getenv(\"OMNIROUTE_BASE_URL\") or os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_UPSTREAM_BASE_URL\")
45|    if not base_url or base_url.strip().lower() in {\"\", \"none\"}:
46|        return None
47|    api_key = os.getenv(\"OMNIROUTE_API_KEY\")
48|    management_token = os.getenv(\"OMNIROUTE_MANAGEMENT_TOKEN\")
49|    usage_api_key_id = os.getenv(\"OMNIROUTE_USAGE_API_KEY_ID\")
50|    allow_private = _allowed_private_hosts() | {
51|        item.strip().lower()
52|        for item in os.getenv(\"OMNIROUTE_ALLOW_PRIVATE_HOSTS\", \"\").split(\",\")
53|        if item.strip()
54|    }
55|    try:
56|        transport = OmniRouteHTTPTransport(
57|            base_url,
58|            api_key=api_key,
59|            management_token=management_token,
60|            usage_api_key_id=usage_api_key_id,
61|            allow_private_hosts=allow_private,
62|        )
63|    except Exception:
64|        return None
65|    adapter = OmniRouteAvailabilityAdapter(transport)
66|    return AvailabilityCache(
67|        source=adapter.evaluate,
68|        ttl_seconds=DEFAULT_AVAILABILITY_TTL_SECONDS,
69|        stale_window_seconds=DEFAULT_AVAILABILITY_STALE_WINDOW_SECONDS,
70|    )
71|
72|
73|DEFAULT_UPSTREAM_BASE_URL = \"http://127.0.0.1:20132/v1\"
74|DEFAULT_MAX_REQUEST_BYTES = 2 * 1024 * 1024
75|DEFAULT_ALLOWED_PRIVATE_HOSTS = {\"127.0.0.1\", \"::1\", \"localhost\"}
76|
77|
78|def _allowed_private_hosts() -> set[str]:
79|    configured = os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_UPSTREAM_ALLOW_PRIVATE_HOSTS\", \"\")
80|    return DEFAULT_ALLOWED_PRIVATE_HOSTS | {
81|        item.strip().lower() for item in configured.split(\",\") if item.strip()
82|    }
83|
84|
85|def _build_proxy() -> UpstreamProxy:
86|    \"\"\"Build the configured upstream transport without reading client fields.\"\"\"
87|    base_url = os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_UPSTREAM_BASE_URL\", DEFAULT_UPSTREAM_BASE_URL)
88|    api_key = os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_UPSTREAM_API_KEY\") or os.getenv(\"OMNIROUTE_API_KEY\")
89|    timeout_ms = int(os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_UPSTREAM_TIMEOUT_MS\", \"30000\"))
90|    if timeout_ms <= 0:
91|        raise ValueError(\"LLMGATE_UPSTREAM_TIMEOUT_MS must be positive\")
92|    return UpstreamProxy(
93|        base_url,
94|        api_key=api_key,
95|        timeout=timeout_ms / 1000,
96|        allow_private_hosts=_allowed_private_hosts(),
97|    )
98|
99|
100|def _build_intelligence() -> IntelligenceService:
101|    \"\"\"Build the public IntelligenceService boundary from environment settings.\"\"\"
102|    profile = os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_INTELLIGENCE_PROFILE\", DEFAULT_PROFILE)
103|    timeout_ms = int(os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_INTELLIGENCE_TIMEOUT_MS\", str(DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS)))
104|    allow_client_model_override = os.getenv(
105|        \"LLMGATE_ALLOW_CLIENT_MODEL_OVERRIDE\", \"false\"
106|    ).lower() in {
107|        \"1\",
108|        \"true\",
109|        \"yes\",
110|        \"on\",
111|    }
112|    frontier_allowlist_raw = os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_FRONTIER_ALLOWLIST\")
113|    frontier_allowlist = (
114|        tuple(item.strip() for item in frontier_allowlist_raw.split(\",\") if item.strip())
115|        if frontier_allowlist_raw
116|        else None
117|    )
118|    providers: dict[str, ProviderConfig] = {}
119|    return IntelligenceService(
120|        primary_model=os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_PRIMARY\", \"anthropic/claude-3-opus-20240229\"),
121|        providers=providers,
122|        profile=profile,
123|        log_path=os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_LOG_PATH\", \"llm-gate-decisions.jsonl\"),
124|        log_full_task=False,
125|        discovery_ttl=int(os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_DISCOVERY_TTL_SECONDS\", \"60\")),
126|        ruflo_command=os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_RUFLO_COMMAND\", \"ruflo\"),
127|        ruvector_command=os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_RUVECTOR_COMMAND\", \"ruvector\"),
128|        timeout_ms=timeout_ms,
129|        frontier_allowlist=frontier_allowlist,
130|        allow_client_model_override=allow_client_model_override,
131|    )
132|
133|
134|@asynccontextmanager
135|async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> Any:
136|    global intelligence_instance, gate_ins
```

### 🔄 Tool result (1784436074.4168475)
```
{"content": "1|\"\"\"Bounded availability cache with documented TTL and stale-while-revalidate.
2|
3|The cache wraps an :class:`OmniRouteAvailabilityAdapter` (or any callable that
4|returns an :class:`AvailabilityReport`).  It deliberately never trusts a stale
5|entry for protected routing unless an explicit policy opt-in allows degraded
6|mode, and it isolates entries per provider/model/policy-version so one
7|source can never poison another.
8|
9|Design constraints (from issue #56 acceptance criteria):
10|- Cache keys include provider/model and the relevant policy/contract version.
11|- Expired or refresh-failed states are explicit ``unknown``/``error`` states.
12|- Concurrent refreshes are bounded and deduplicated.
13|- ``explain`` output identifies freshness and source.
14|\"\"\"
15|
16|from __future__ import annotations
17|
18|import threading
19|from collections.abc import Callable
20|from dataclasses import dataclass, field, replace
21|from datetime import datetime, timedelta
22|from typing import Any
23|
24|from llm_gate.availability import AvailabilityReport, _now
25|
26|
27|@dataclass(frozen=True)
28|class CacheKey:
29|    \"\"\"Identity for a cached availability observation.
30|
31|    Includes provider/model and the policy/contract version so a contract
32|    change invalidates stale entries instead of leaking them across versions.
33|    \"\"\"
34|
35|    provider: str
36|    model: str
37|    policy_version: str = \"policy-2026-07-13.1\"
38|
39|    @classmethod
40|    def for_candidate(cls, model_id: str, policy_version: str | None = None) -> CacheKey:
41|        provider = model_id.split(\"/\", 1)[0] if \"/\" in model_id else \"unknown\"
42|        return cls(
43|            provider=provider,
44|            model=model_id,
45|            policy_version=policy_version or cls.policy_version,
46|        )
47|
48|
49|@dataclass
50|class _Entry:
51|    \"\"\"Mutable cache entry holding the last good report and refresh bookkeeping.\"\"\"
52|
53|    report: AvailabilityReport
54|    stored_at: datetime
55|    ttl_seconds: int
56|    # Refresh coordination: only one in-flight refresh per key at a time.
57|    refreshing: bool = False
58|    refresh_error: str | None = None
59|    last_refresh_attempt: datetime | None = None
60|
61|    def age_seconds(self, now: datetime) -> float:
62|        return (now - self.stored_at).total_seconds()
63|
64|    def is_fresh(self, now: datetime, *, stale_window_seconds: int) -> bool:
65|        # Freshness is purely TTL-based; the stale window governs revalidation.
66|        return self.age_seconds(now) <= self.ttl_seconds
67|
68|    def is_within_stale_window(self, now: datetime, *, stale_window_seconds: int) -> bool:
69|        age = self.age_seconds(now)
70|        return self.ttl_seconds < age <= (self.ttl_seconds + stale_window_seconds)
71|
72|
73|def _default_clock() -> datetime:
74|    return _now()
75|
76|
77|def _confidence_for(report: AvailabilityReport) -> float:
78|    \"\"\"Deterministic, secret-free confidence in [0, 1] for an explain record.
79|
80|    Errors collapse confidence to zero. The cache/replay source (``\"cache\"``)
81|    is treated as low-trust. Otherwise confidence tracks the eligible share of
82|    observed candidates.
83|    \"\"\"
84|    if report.errors:
85|        return 0.0
86|    if not report.candidates:
87|        return 0.0 if report.source in (\"unknown\", \"cache\") else 0.5
88|    ratio = len(report.eligible) / len(report.candidates)
89|    if report.source in (\"unknown\", \"cache\"):
90|        return round(0.3 * ratio, 3)
91|    return round(ratio, 3)
92|
93|
94|@dataclass
95|class AvailabilityCache:
96|    \"\"\"Bounded, per-key availability cache with stale-while-revalidate.
97|
98|    ``get`` returns a cached report when fresh.  When the entry is within
99|    the stale window it returns the stale report (so callers keep working)
100|    but triggers a deduplicated refresh for next time.  When the entry is
101|    past the stale window (or absent) it performs a synchronous refresh and,
102|    on failure, returns an explicit ``unknown`` report rather than silently
103|    serving poisoned data.
104|    \"\"\"
105|
106|    source: Callable[[], AvailabilityReport]
107|    ttl_seconds: int = 60
108|    stale_window_seconds: int = 30
109|    policy_version: str = \"policy-2026-07-13.1\"
110|    clock: Callable[[], datetime] | None = None
111|    max_entries: int = 4096
112|    # Reentrant lock: ``get`` may call ``_refresh_locked`` while already holding
113|    # the lock (nested acquisition), so a plain ``Lock`` would deadlock.
114|    _entries: dict[CacheKey, _Entry] = field(default_factory=dict)
115|    _lock: threading.RLock = field(default_factory=threading.RLock)
116|
117|    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
118|        if not isinstance(self.ttl_seconds, int) or self.ttl_seconds <= 0:
119|            raise ValueError(\"ttl_seconds must be a positive integer\")
120|        if not isinstance(self.stale_window_seconds, int) or self.stale_window_seconds < 0:
121|            raise ValueError(\"stale_window_seconds must be a non-negative integer\")
122|        if not isinstance(self.max_entries, int) or self.max_entries < 1:
123|            raise ValueError(\"max_entries must be a positive integer\")
124|        # Reject a non-callable source early; the adapter is required.
125|        if not callable(self.source):
126|            raise TypeError(\"source must be callable and return an AvailabilityReport\")
127|
128|    def _key(self, model_id: str) -> CacheKey:
129|        return CacheKey.for_candidate(model_id, self.policy_version)
130|
131|    def get(self, model_id: str) -> AvailabilityReport:
132|        \"\"\"Return a cached-or-refreshed report for ``model_id``.
133|
134|        Fresh -> cached.  Stale-but-within-window -> cached + schedule refresh.
135|        Expired/missing -> refresh now; on refresh failure return explicit
136|        ``unknown`` rather than the stale entry.
137|        \
```

## 🤖 Assistant (1784436074.4168484)

I now have complete command of the code. The honest finding: **`intelligence.route()` (the live routing path) never consults availability at all** — it picks `best_model = select_best_model(candidates, final_tier, ...)` purely from the catalog, and `select_best_model` only filters on `capability_tier` + `is_available` (a static catalog flag). The `AvailabilityCache` from #56 is wired only into the explain endpoint. That is exactly the #57/#72 gap: the routing path does not filter by live eligibility before ranking.

The minimal, doc-compliant fix (reusing existing pieces, no reinvention):

1. Add an `EligibilityGate` that, given candidate `ModelInfo` + the `AvailabilityCache`, (a) drops any candidate whose cached report state is not eligible/ready, (b) for **protected** work fails closed when the cached state is `unknown`/`error`/missing. This is the single source of truth both router and explain use.
2. Wire `EligibilityGate` into `IntelligenceService.route()` BEFORE `select_best_model` — satisfying "filter before ranking in every route path." Also reflect the gate's `eligible`/`exclusions` into the `RoutingDecision` (preserving exclusions per AC).
3. Extend `/v1/route/explain` (#73) to return the full candidate set, pre-ranking eligible set, per-candidate exclusion reasons, and cache confidence/refresh_error — from the same gate.
4. Probe wiring (#57 root cause): make `_build_availability_cache` optionally enrich the adapter with a `ProbeRunner` when `LLMGATE_AVAILABILITY_PROFILE=production` and a probe base URL/key are set — reusing `ProbeRunner`/`openai_probe_transport`, no new transport code.

Let me create a new `eligibility.py` module (the gate) and a probe-enriched adapter in `availability.py`, then wire them. I'll start the isolated worktree per the runbook to avoid conflicts, implement, and run the full test suite + ruff + mypy before reporting. Let me set up the worktree and write the gate module.

```
[Subdirectory context discovered: llm-gate-57/AGENTS.md]
# AI Agent Constraints & Architectural Context (.claude.md)

**Target Ecosystem:** Express.js Middleware / Next.js Routing
**Language:** Python 3.10+
**Primary Directive:** LLM API Cost Optimization and Fallback Orchestration.

## Directory Boundaries
- `/llm_gate/gate.py`: Core routing engine. Modifying heuristic logic here requires validating the execution tree in `tests/test_gate_unit.py`.
- `/llm_gate/discovery.py`: The integration connector. Only add OpenRouter/Ollama parsers here. 
- `/llm_gate/api.py`: FastAPI server layer. Must never be instantiated in global execution scopes.

## RAG Memory & Constraints
If an agent edits the parsing logic in `models.py`, ensure data structures remain mapped as `dataclass(frozen=True)` to prevent mutation across asynchronous generation loops.

## Deployment Gating
Do not update `pyproject.toml` dependencies natively without also checking `[server]` and `[ci]` optional blocks. `llm-gate` is headless by design.
```

```json
{
 "path": "/home/nick/dev/llm-gate-57/llm_gate/eligibility.py",
 "content": "\"\"\"Single-source-of-truth eligibility gate for pre-ranking filtering.\n\nThis module implements the issue #57 / #72 invariant: candidate filtering\nhappens *before* any adaptive or cost ranking, and no ranker, Ruflo plan, or\nRuVector result can reintroduce a candidate that the gate excluded.\n\nThe gate consults the already-merged :class:`AvailabilityCache` (issue #56),\nwhich wraps the :class:`OmniRouteAvailabilityAdapter`.  It is deliberately\nprotocol-based: it takes a ``Callable[[str], AvailabilityReport]`` so the live\nrouting path and the explain endpoint share identical truth.\n\nFail-closed semantics (per ROUTING_POLICY + #57 AC): when a request is\n*protected* and the live availability truth is absent (``unknown`` / ``error``\n/ missing), the candidate is excluded rather than optimistically admitted.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, Mapping\n\nfrom llm_gate.availability import AvailabilityState, AvailabilityReport\nfrom llm_gate.models import ModelInfo\n\n\nclass EligibilityVerdict(str, Enum):\n    \"\"\"Why a candidate was kept or excluded by the gate.\"\"\"\n\n    ELIGIBLE = \"eligible\"\n    NOT_LIVE_ELIGIBLE = \"not_live_eligible\"\n    RUNTIME_TRUTH_ABSENT = \"runtime_truth_absent\"\n    NOT_REQUESTED_TIER = \"not_requested_tier\"\n\n\n# States that admit a candidate into the pre-ranking eligible set.\n_ADMITTED_STATES = frozenset(\n    {\n        AvailabilityState.ELIGIBLE,\n        AvailabilityState.READY,\n        AvailabilityState.DEGRADED,\n    }\n)\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass EligibilityRecord:\n    \"\"\"Per-candidate gate outcome, preserved for the explain endpoint (#73).\"\"\"\n\n    model_id: str\n    provider: str\n    admitted: bool\n    verdict: str\n    state: str\n    source: str\n    reason: str | None = None\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        return {\n            \"model_id\": self.model_id,\n            \"provider\": self.provider,\n            \"admitted\": self.admitted,\n            \"verdict\": self.verdict.value if isinstance(self.verdict, EligibilityVerdict) else self.verdict,\n            \"state\": self.state,\n            \"source\": self.source,\n            \"reason\": self.reason,\n        }\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EligibilityResult:\n    \"\"\"Outcome of filtering a candidate set before ranking.\"\"\"\n\n    admitted: list[ModelInfo] = field(default_factory=list)\n    records: list[EligibilityRecord] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n    @property\n    def exclusions(self) -> list[EligibilityRecord]:\n        return [r for r in self.records if not r.admitted]\n\n    @property\n    def eligible(self) -> list[ModelInfo]:\n        return self.admitted\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        return {\n            \"admitted\": [m.id for m in self.admitted],\n            \"records\": [r.to_dict() for r in self.records],\n            \"exclusions\": [r.to_dict() for r in self.exclusions],\n        }\n\n\ndef _state_for(report: AvailabilityReport | None, model_id: str) -> tuple[str, str]:\n    \"\"\"Return (state, source) for a model from its cached report.\"\"\"\n    if report is None:\n        return (\"unknown\", \"cache\")\n    for candidate in report.candidates:\n        if candidate.model.id == model_id or candidate.model.id.split(\"/\", 1)[-1] == model_id.split(\"/\", 1)[-1]:\n            return (candidate.state.value, candidate.source)\n    # Candidate absent from the report entirely -> treat as unknown (fail-closed\n    # for protected work; the router may still admit unverified in dev mode).\n    return (\"unknown\", report.source)\n\n\nclass EligibilityGate:\n    \"\"\"Filter candidates by 243|    def __init__(self, operations: Mapping[str, Any]) -> None:
```
```
244|        self._operations = dict(operations)

246|        if unsupported:
247|            raise OmniRouteTransportUnsupported(
248|                unsupported[0],
249|                f\"unsupported operation; expected one of {', '.join(SUPPORTED_TRANSPORT_OPERATIONS)}\",

252|            operation
253|            for operation, names in (

256|                (\"discover_capabilities\", CAPABILITY_TRANSPORT_OPERATIONS),
257|            )
258|            if any(name in self._operations for name in names)
259|        )
260|
261|    def _resolve(self, *names: str) -> Any:
262|        for name in names:
263|            if name not in self._operations:
264|                continue
265|            value = self._operations[name]
266|            return value() if callable(value) else value
267|        raise OmniRouteTransportUnsupported(names[0], f\"expected one of {', '.join(names)}\")

274|
```

## 🤖 Assistant (1784436074.4168599)

I have the full `normalize_observation` / `_probe_state` contract. Now I'll add a `ProbeEnrichedAdapter` to `availability.py` that wraps the base adapter's `evaluate()` and, when probing is enabled, runs `ProbeRunner` and merges probe observations into the candidate states. This reuses all existing primitives — `ProbeRunner`, `openai_probe_transport`, `normalize_observation`, `_probe_state`. Inserting before the `adapter_from_transport` factory at the end.

> 🔧 **tool_call**: `patch`
```json
{
 "mode": "replace",
 "path": "/home/nick/dev/llm-gate-57/llm_gate/availability.py",
 "old_string": "    check = evaluate\n\n\ndef adapter_from_transport(",
 "new_string": "    check = evaluate\n\n\nclass ProbeEnrichedAdapter:\n    \"\"\"Adapter that adds bounded live probes on top of catalog/runtime truth.\n\n    This is the issue #57 root-cause wiring: the existing\n    :class:`OmniRouteAvailabilityAdapter` only consults catalog + runtime\n    metadata, never proving a model is actually usable.  This adapter reuses the\n    already-built, already-tested :class:`~llm_gate.probes.ProbeRunner` and\n    merges single-token probe observations into the same candidate states the\n    router consumes.  No new transport code is introduced: the probe uses the\n    documented ``openai_probe_transport`` (POST /v1/chat/completions), separate\n    from the GET-only catalog/runtime transport.\n\n    Probing is opt-in via ``enabled`` so the development/default profile stays\n    pure catalog/runtime (per ENFORCEMENT_AND_LEARNING: probing is a\n    production-profile concern).  When disabled the adapter is a thin pass-through\n    to the wrapped adapter.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        base: OmniRouteAvailabilityAdapter,\n        *,\n        probe_transport: Any | None = None,\n        enabled: bool = False,\n        policy: Any | None = None,\n        registry: Any | None = None,\n        max_probed: int = 16,\n        clock: Any = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self.base = base\n        self.probe_transport = probe_transport\n        self.enabled = enabled\n        self._runner = ProbeRunner(policy=policy, registry=registry) if enabled else None\n        self.max_probed = max_probed\n        self.clock = clock\n\n    def evaluate(\n        self,\n        requirements: CandidateRequirements = CandidateRequirements(),\n        *,\n        now: datetime | None = None,\n    ) -> AvailabilityReport:\n        report = self.base.evaluate(requirements, now=now)\n        if not self.enabled or self._runner is None or self.probe_transport is None:\n            return report\n        # Bound the probe fan-out to the most relevant candidates to keep the\n        # one-token probe cheap and within ProbePolicy.max_models_per_run.\n        model_ids = [c.model.id for c in report.candidates][: self.max_probed]\n        if not model_ids:\n            return report\n        observations = self._runner.run(model_ids, self.probe_transport, now=now)\n        probe_by_id: dict[str, RuntimeObservation] = {}\n        for obs in observations:\n            try:\n                probe_by_id[obs.model_id] = obs.as_runtime_observation()\n            except Exception:\n                # A malformed probe result must never poison the report.\n                continue\n        if not probe_by_id:\n            return report\n        current = _now(now or (self.clock() if self.clock else None))\n        merged = [\n            self._merge_probe(candidate, probe_by_id, current)\n            for candidate in report.candidates\n        ]\n        eligible = select_capable_candidates(merged, requirements)\n        return AvailabilityReport(\n            tuple(merged),\n            tuple(eligible),\n            report.source,\n            report.freshness_seconds,\n            report.errors,\n        )\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _merge_probe(\n        candidate: AvailabilityCandidate,\n        probe_by_id: dict[str, RuntimeObservation],\n        now: datetime,\n    ) -> AvailabilityCandidate:\n        probe = probe_by_id.get(candidate.model.id)\n        if probe is None:\n            return candidate\n        # Probe truth is authoritative when it contradicts catalog/runtime.\n        probe_candidate = normalize_observation(candidate.model, probe, now=now)\n        if probe_candidate.state is AvailabilityState.MALFORMED:\n            # @@ -1269,6 +1269,100 @@
```
```
     check = evaluate
 
 
+class ProbeEnrichedAdapter:
+    \"\"\"Adapter that adds bounded live probes on top of catalog/runtime truth.
+
+    This is the issue #57 root-cause wiring: the existing
+    :class:`OmniRouteAvailabilityAdapter` only consults catalog + runtime
+    metadata, never proving a model is actually usable.  This adapter reuses the
+    already-built, already-tested :class:`~llm_gate.probes.ProbeRunner` and
+    merges single-token probe observations into the same candidate states the
+    router consumes.  No new transport code is introduced: the probe uses the
+    documented ``openai_probe_transport`` (POST /v1/chat/completions), separate
+    from the GET-only catalog/runtime transport.
+
+    Probing is opt-in via ``enabled`` so the development/default profile stays
+    pure catalog/runtime (per ENFORCEMENT_AND_LEARNING: probing is a
+    production-profile concern).  When disabled the adapter is a thin pass-through
+    to the wrapped adapter.
+    \"\"\"
+
+    def __init__(
+        self,
+        base: OmniRouteAvailabilityAdapter,
+        *,
+        probe_transport: Any | None = None,
+        enabled: bool = False,
+        policy: Any | None = None,
+        registry: Any | None = None,
+        max_probed: int = 16,
+        clock: Any = None,
+    ) -> None:
+        self.base = base
+        self.probe_transport = probe_transport
+        self.enabled = enabled
+        self._runner = ProbeRunner(policy=policy, registry=registry) if enabled else None
+        self.max_probed = max_probed
+        self.clock = clock
+
+    def evaluate(
+        self,
+        requirements: CandidateRequirements = CandidateRequirements(),
+        *,
+        now: datetime | None = None,
+    ) -> AvailabilityReport:
+        report = self.base.evaluate(requirements, now=now)
+        if not self.enabled or self._runner is None or self.probe_transport is None:
+            return report
+        # Bound the probe fan-out to the most relevant candidates to keep the
+        # one-token probe cheap and within ProbePolicy.max_models_per_run.
+        model_ids = [c.model.id for c in report.candidates][: self.max_probed]
+        if not model_ids:
+            return report
+        observations = self._runner.run(model_ids, self.probe_transport, now=now)
+        probe_by_id: dict[str, RuntimeObservation] = {}
+        for obs in observations:
+            try:
+                probe_by_id[obs.model_id] = obs.as_runtime_observation()
+            except Exception:
+                # A malformed probe result must never poison the report.
+                continue
+        if not probe_by_id:
+            return report
+        current = _now(now or (self.clock() if self.clock else None))
+        merged = [
+            self._merge_probe(candidate, probe_by_id, current)
+            for candidate in report.candidates
+        ]
+        eligible = select_capable_candidates(merged, requirements)
+        return AvailabilityReport(
+            tuple(merged),
+            tuple(eligible),
+            report.source,
+            report.freshness_seconds,
+            report.errors,
+        )
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def _merge_probe(
+        candidate: AvailabilityCandidate,
+        probe_by_id: dict[str, RuntimeObservation],
+        now: datetime,
+    ) -> AvailabilityCandidate:
+        probe = probe_by_id.get(candidate.model.id)
+        if probe is None:
+            return candidate
+        # Probe truth is authoritative when it contradicts catalog/runtime.
+        probe_candidate = normalize_observation(candidate.model, probe, now=now)
+        if probe_candidate.state is AvailabilityState.MALFORMED:
+            # Keep the catalog/runtime verdict rather than degrading on a probe
+            # metadata glitch.
+            return candidate
+        return probe_candidate
+
+    check = evaluate
+

 ) -> OmniRouteAvailabilityAdapter:
```

# Client SDKs

> Lightweight, type-safe clients for the OpenRouter API

The Client SDKs give you a thin, type-safe layer over the OpenRouter REST API. It handles authentication, request validation, and response typing so you can call any of 400+ models with a single function call — no boilerplate, no provider-specific quirks.

## Installation

| Language   | Package                                                            |
| ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| TypeScript | [`@openrouter/sdk`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@openrouter/sdk) |
| Python     | [`openrouter`](https://pypi.org/project/openrouter/)               |
| Go         | [`go-sdk`](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/OpenRouterTeam/go-sdk)    |

<CodeGroup>
  ```bash title="npm" lines theme={null}
  npm install @openrouter/sdk
  ```

  ```bash title="pnpm" lines theme={null}
  pnpm add @openrouter/sdk
  ```

  ```bash title="yarn" lines theme={null}
  yarn add @openrouter/sdk
  ```

  ```bash title="bun" lines theme={null}
  bun add @openrouter/sdk
  ```

  ```bash title="deno" lines theme={null}
  deno add npm:@openrouter/sdk
  ```

  ```bash title="pip" lines theme={null}
  pip install openrouter
  ```

  ```bash title="go" lines theme={null}
  go get github.com/OpenRouterTeam/go-sdk
  ```
</CodeGroup>

All three SDKs are auto-generated from the OpenRouter OpenAPI spec, so new models, parameters, and endpoints appear immediately after each API release.

## When to use the Client SDKs

Choose the Client SDKs when you need **direct, efficient access to model inference** and want to manage your own application logic:

* **Single-turn completions** — send a prompt, get a response
* **Streaming responses** — real-time token-by-token output
* **Embeddings, video, and rerank** — generate vector representations, create videos, and rerank results
* **API key and credit management** — programmatic control over your account
* **Custom orchestration** — you handle conversation loops, tool dispatch, and state yourself

The Client SDKs are intentionally lean. It mirrors the OpenRouter API surface 1:1 with full type safety, so there is no abstraction to fight when you need fine-grained control.

<Tip>
  If you want higher-level primitives for building agents — multi-turn loops, tool definitions, stop conditions, and conversation state management — see the [Agent SDK](/agent-sdk/overview) instead.
</Tip>

## Quick example

<CodeGroup>
  ```typescript title="TypeScript" lines theme={null}
  import { OpenRouter } from '@openrouter/sdk';

  const client = new OpenRouter({
    apiKey: process.env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY,
  });

  const response = await client.chat.send({
    model: 'openai/gpt-5.2',
    messages: [
      { role: 'user', content: 'Explain quantum computing in one sentence.' },
    ],
  });

  console.log(response.choices[0].message.content);
  ```

  ```python title="Python" lines theme={null}
  from openrouter import OpenRouter
  import os

  with OpenRouter(api_key=os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY")) as client:
      response = client.chat.send(
          model="openai/gpt-5.2",
          messages=[
              {"role": "user", "content": "Explain quantum computing in one sentence."}
          ],
      )

      print(response.choices[0].message.content)
  ```
</CodeGroup>

## Client SDKs vs Agent SDK

|                        | Client SDKs                                                   | Agent SDK                                                           |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Focus**              | Lean API client — mirrors the REST API with full type safety  | Agentic primitives — multi-turn loops, tools, stop conditions       |
| **Use when**           | You want direct model calls and manage orchestration yourself | You want built-in agent loops, tool execution, and state management |
| **Conversation state** | You manage it                                                 | Managed for you via `callModel`                                     |
| **Tool execution**     | You dispatch tool calls                                       | Automatic with the `tool()` helper                                  |
| **Languages**          | TypeScript, Python, Go                                        | TypeScript                                                          |

## Next steps

* [TypeScript SDK reference](/client-sdks/typescript)
* [Python SDK reference](/client-sdks/python)
* [Go SDK reference](/client-sdks/go)
* [Agent SDK overview](/agent-sdk/overview) — for building agents with multi-turn loops and tools - https://openrouter.ai/docs/client-sdks/overview.md, and agents-sdk https://openrouter.ai/docs/agent-sdk/overview.md - 
> ## Documentation Index
> Fetch the complete documentation index at: https://openrouter.ai/docs/llms.txt
> Use this file to discover all available pages before exploring further.

# Agent SDK

> Build AI agents with multi-turn loops, tools, and conversation state

The Agent SDK (`@openrouter/agent`) provides the primitives you need to build agentic applications on OpenRouter. Instead of manually wiring up conversation loops, tool dispatch, and state tracking, the Agent SDK handles all of that so you can focus on defining *what* your agent does.

The Agent SDK is built to work alongside the [Client SDKs](/client-sdks/overview). Installing `@openrouter/agent` automatically includes the Client SDKs as well, but each package can work independently.

## When to use the Agent SDK

Choose the Agent SDK when you need **agentic behavior** — multi-step reasoning where the model calls tools, processes results, and decides what to do next:

* **Multi-turn agent loops** — `callModel` automatically loops until a stop condition is met
* **Tool definitions** — define tools with the `tool()` helper and the SDK executes them for you
* **Stop conditions** — control when the loop ends with `stepCountIs`, `hasToolCall`, `maxCost`, and more
* **Conversation state** — the SDK tracks messages, tool results, and context across turns
* **Streaming** — real-time token output within each agent step
* **Dynamic parameters** — change model, temperature, or tools between turns based on context

<Tip>
  If you only need simple request/response calls to a model without agent loops, the [Client SDKs](/client-sdks/overview) are a lighter-weight option.
</Tip>

## Installation

<CodeGroup>
  ```bash title="npm" lines theme={null}
  npm install @openrouter/agent
  ```

  ```bash title="pnpm" lines theme={null}
  pnpm add @openrouter/agent
  ```

  ```bash title="yarn" lines theme={null}
  yarn add @openrouter/agent
  ```

  ```bash title="bun" lines theme={null}
  bun add @openrouter/agent
  ```

  ```bash title="deno" lines theme={null}
  deno add npm:@openrouter/agent
  ```
</CodeGroup>

## Quick example

```typescript expandable lines theme={null}
import { OpenRouter, tool } from '@openrouter/agent';
import { z } from 'zod';

const openrouter = new OpenRouter({
  apiKey: process.env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY,
});

const weatherTool = tool({
  name: 'get_weather',
  description: 'Get the current weather for a location',
  inputSchema: z.object({
    location: z.string().describe('City name'),
  }),
  execute: async ({ location }) => {
    return { temperature: 72, condition: 'sunny', location };
  },
});

const result = openrouter.callModel({
  model: 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4',
  messages: [
    { role: 'user', content: 'What is the weather in San Francisco?' },
  ],
  tools: [weatherTool],
});

const text = await result.getText();
console.log(text);
```

The SDK sends the message to the model, receives a tool call, executes `get_weather`, feeds the result back, and returns the final response — all in one `callModel` invocation.

## Core concepts

### `callModel`

The main entry point. It runs an inference loop that:

1. Sends messages to the model
2. If the model returns tool calls, executes them automatically
3. Appends tool results to the conversation
4. Repeats until a stop condition is met or no more tool calls are made

See the [Call Model documentation](/agent-sdk/call-model) for the full API.

### Tools

Define tools with the `tool()` helper. Each tool has a name, description, Zod parameter schema, and an `execute` function. The SDK handles serialization, validation, and dispatch.

```typescript lines theme={null}
import { tool } from '@openrouter/agent';
import { z } from 'zod';

const searchTool = tool({
  name: 'search',
  description: 'Search the web',
  inputSchema: z.object({ query: z.string() }),
  execute: async ({ query }) => {
    // Your search implementation
    return { results: ['...'] };
  },
});
```

### Stop conditions

Control when the agent loop terminates:

```typescript lines theme={null}
import { OpenRouter, stepCountIs, maxCost } from '@openrouter/agent';

const openrouter = new OpenRouter({
  apiKey: process.env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY,
});

const result = openrouter.callModel({
  model: 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4',
  messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Research this topic thoroughly' }],
  tools: [searchTool],
  stopWhen: [stepCountIs(10), maxCost(0.50)],
});
```

## Agent SDK vs Client SDKs

|                        | Agent SDK                                                           | Client SDKs                                                   |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Focus**              | Agentic primitives — multi-turn loops, tools, stop conditions       | Lean API client — mirrors the REST API with full type safety  |
| **Use when**           | You want built-in agent loops, tool execution, and state management | You want direct model calls and manage orchestration yourself |
| **Conversation state** | Managed for you via `callModel`                                     | You manage it                                                 |
| **Tool execution**     | Automatic with the `tool()` helper                                  | You dispatch tool calls                                       |
| **Languages**          | TypeScript                                                          | TypeScript, Python, Go                                        |

## Next steps

* [Call Model](/agent-sdk/call-model) — the complete `callModel` API reference
* [Tools](/agent-sdk/call-model/tools) — defining and using tools
* [Stop Conditions](/agent-sdk/call-model/stop-conditions) — controlling agent loop termination
* [Streaming](/agent-sdk/call-model/streaming) — real-time token output
* [DevTools](/agent-sdk/dev-tools/devtools) — telemetry capture and visualization for development
* [Migrating from @openrouter/sdk](/agent-sdk/agent-migration) — move agent imports to the standalone package, api reference - https://openrouter.ai/docs/api_reference/overview all docs - https://openrouter.ai/docs/quickstart....

Reuses ProbeRunner + the\n    # documented openai_probe_transport; disabled by default (development).\n    probe_base_url = os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_PROBE_BASE_URL\")\n    probe_enabled = os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_AVAILABILITY_PROFILE\", \"development\").lower() == \"production\"\n    if probe_enabled and probe_base_url:\n        from llm_gate.availability import ProbeEnrichedAdapter\n        from llm_gate.probes import openai_probe_transport\n\n        probe_transport = openai_probe_transport(\n            probe_base_url,\n            api_key=os.getenv(\"LLMGATE_PROBE_API_KEY\") or api_key,\n        )\n        adapter = ProbeEnrichedAdapter(\n            adapter, probe_transport=probe_transport, enabled=True\n        )\n    cache = AvailabilityCache(\n        source=adapter.evaluate,\n        ttl_seconds=DEFAULT_AVAILABILITY_TTL_SECONDS,\n        stale_window_seconds=DEFAULT_AVAILABILITY_STALE_WINDOW_SECONDS,\n    )\n    from llm_gate.eligibility import EligibilityGate\n\n    gate = EligibilityGate(\n        cache.get,\n        protected_fail_closed=True,\n        allow_unverified_in_dev=True,\n    )\n    return cache, gate",

Now extend `/v1/route/explain` (#73) to return the full candidate set, pre-ranking eligible set, per-candidate exclusions, and cache confidence. Let me patch the explain endpoint.

> 🔧 **tool_call**: `patch`
```json
{
 "mode": "replace",
 "new_string": "@app.get(\"/v1/route/explain\")\nasync def route_explain(model_id: str | None = None) -> Response:\n    \"\"\"Explain availability freshness and eligibility for one or all cached models.\n\n    Implements the issue #56 / #73 explain contract: surfaces observed_at,\n    expires_at, age, source, confidence, candidate/eligible counts, per-candidate\n    exclusion reasons (#73), and cache refresh/error state (``cache_state``,\n    ``stale``, ``refreshing``, ``refresh_error``).\n\n    Without ``model_id`` the response reports the cache scope (policy version and\n    configured model keys) plus the gate's pre-ranking eligible/exclusion sets\n    when available. With ``model_id`` it returns the per-model freshness explain\n    record, refreshing on first access.\n    \"\"\"\n    if availability_cache_instance is None:\n        return _proxy_error(\n            503,\n            \"availability cache not configured (set OMNIROUTE_BASE_URL to enable)\",\n        )\n    if model_id is None or model_id == \"\":\n        base = {\n            \"policy_version\": availability_cache_instance.policy_version,\n            \"cached_models\": sorted(availability_cache_instance.keys()),\n            \"cache_state\": \"configured\",\n        }\n        # Issue #73: surface the gate's complete pre-ranking eligible set and\n        # exclusions from the same authority the router uses.\n        if eligibility_gate_instance is not None:\n            gate_eval = eligibility_gate_instance.evaluate(\n                [\n                    ModelInfo(id=mid, provider=mid.split(\"/\", 1)[0] if \"/\" in mid else \"unknown\")\n                    for mid in base[\"cached_models\"]\n                ],\n                dev_mode=True,\n            )\n            base[\"eligible_set\"] = [m.id for m in gate_eval.eligible]\n            base[\"exclusions\"] = [\n                r.to_dict() for r in gate_eval.exclusions\n            ]\n        return JSONResponse(content=base)\n    record = availability_cache_instance.explain(model_id)\n    if eligibility_gate_instance is not None:\n        from llm_gate.models import ModelInfo\n\n        gate_eval = eligibility_gate_instance.evaluate(\n            [\n                ModelInfo(\n                    id=model_id,\n                    provider=model_id.split(\"/\", 1)[0] if \"/\" in model_id else \"unknown\",\n                )\n            ],\n            dev_mode=True,\n        )\n        if gate_eval.records:\n            record[\"eligibility\"] = gate_eval.records[0].to_dict()\n            record[\"eligible\"] = gate_eval.records[0].admitted\n    return JSONResponse(content=record)",
 "old_string": "@app.get(\"/v1/route/explain\")\nasync def route_explain(model_id: str | None = None) -> Response:\n    \"\"\"Explain availability freshness and source for one or all cached models.\n\n    Implements the issue #56 explain contract: surfaces observed_at, expires_at,\n    age, source, confidence, candidate/eligible counts, and cache refresh/error\n    state (``cache_state``, ``stale``, ``refreshing``, ``refresh_error``).\n\n    Without ``model_id`` the response reports the cache scope (policy version and\n    configured model keys). With ``model_id`` it returns the per-model freshness\n    explain record, refreshing on first access.\n    \"\"\"\n    if availability_cache_instance is None:\n        return _proxy_error(\n            503,\n            \"availability cache not configured (set OMNIROUTE_BASE_URL to enable)\",\n        )\n    if model_id is None or model_id == \"\":\n        return JSONResponse(\n            content={\n                \"policy_version\": availability_cache_instance.policy_version,\n                \"cached_models\": sorted(availability_cache_instance.keys()),\n                \"cache_state\": \"configured\",\n            }\n        )\n    record = availability_cache_instance.explain(model_id)\n    return JSONResponse(content=record)",
```

- Never preserve API keys/tokens/credentials — use [REDACTED].
- Must pass ruff + mypy --strict + pytest.
- KISS/DRY, match surrounding style, elitist/shorthand/clever/concise before commit.
- Autonomous driving permitted; no mid-task questions unless blocked.
- Atomic loop (runbook): worktree per slice → focused+full tests → independent review → fix → atomic commit → ff-only integrate → push → exact-SHA CI watch → update issue/Project → retain Hindsight checkpoint.
- Fail-closed: never dispatch to unproven workers; OmniRoute/9router must pass canary first.
- NEW protocol (2026-07-19): audit all tools → research best patterns → ingest/parse ALL docs entirely before any decision/code → reuse existing tools first → store docs to memory (Hindsight) for future → then implement.
- NEW: Hermes role = orchestrate/research/design/verify; delegate piece-execution to swarms/agents/cloud.
- NEW: code-review-graph to be leveraged whenever possible (CLI-based review/blast-radius/risk).
- NEW: OmniRoute = diegosouzapw/OmniRoute (free AI gateway, 265 providers, 90+ free, auto-fallback); repo cloned to /tmp/OmniRoute for doc ingestion.
- NEW FOCUS: free unlimited model — no concern about turn/spend budget; read everything needed for simultaneous understanding; use gh lib to pull repos; review ALL openrouter.ai docs and suggest repo improvements; clarify auto-routing model scope; NO hardcoded workers/tiers — use dynamic returned list + ruflo neural/SORA patterns.
- CRITICAL USER CORRECTION: Codex workers unavailable (version pin regression); do NOT use ruflo `agent spawn --provider openrouter` (hardcoded); leverage dynamic model list from OmniRoute catalog; assign best-available model per task criticality via ruflo neural/SORA documented patterns; stop reinventing.
- OpenRouter docs: fetch https://openrouter.ai/docs/llms.txt as index, then read all pages; SDKs are OpenAI-compatible → spitball pointing them at http://localhost:20128.

## Key Decisions
- Use OmniRouteHTTPTransport (native, loopback-only, GET-only) as AvailabilityReport source; probes need separate POST transport (openai_probe_transport in probes.py).

- Fail-closed: 503 if OmniRoute unset.
- Do NOT merge codex/* destructive branches.
- Codex bank reachable via API (bankId "codex"); my tools locked to "hermes" bank.
- Runbook + codex bank reconciled (commit c9f6c00 + retain).
- NEW: Hermes = orchestrator; delegate execution to swarms/agents/cloud via functions; workers through OmniRoute dynamic catalog (NO hardcoded provider/tier).
- NEW: Before any code — audit tools, read ALL docs entirely, store compact synthesis to Hindsight (NOT bulk ingest per MEMORY_SOURCE_INDEX), reuse existing tools first.
- NEW: code-review-graph CLI (graph 764 nodes) for review/PR.
- OmniRoute usability = /v1/models (api key) + /api/monitoring/health (NO-auth) + probe POST (api key).
- CORRECTION: "use vectors/insights to reflect" = ruflo SPARC skill, not Hindsight. Hindsight doc store via /files/retain exists; bulk ingest forbidden.
- OmniRoute repo = diegosouzapw/OmniRoute; real ruflo source = ruvnet/claude-flow (cloned /tmp/claude-flow).

- Ownership model: Hermes authors tickets/AC/state + verifies; workers implement to spec via OmniRoute dynamic model selection (ruflo neural/SORA patterns).
- Auto-routing finding: classifier.py applies 4-tier regex to EVERY catalog model (dynamic), unmatched→tier2; NOT a fixed Claude-only allowlist. Real gap = static regex upkeep, fixable via OpenRouter custom classifiers / OmniRoute server-side smart-routing.

- openrouter.ai — quickstart, subagent server-tool, and the full doc index (llms.txt)

---

YOUR AUTO-ROUTING CLAIM — verified against actual code, and it's a PARTIAL misunderstanding. Here's the honest finding:

llm-gate does NOT "only use Claude opus/haiku/sonnet." What it actually does (classifier.py:47 + catalog.py:70):
- It fetches the FULL dynamic catalog from OmniRoute/OpenRouter /v1/models (3,628 models in our case).
- It applies classify(model_id) to EVERY model — a 4-tier regex table (T0 opus/gpt-5.5/grok-4/o3-pro; T1 sonnet-4/gpt-4o/…; T2 sonnet-3/gpt-4o-mini/haiku-3.5/llama-70b/…; T3 haiku/flash/mini/nano/…).
- Unmatched models default to tier 2 (medium), so nothing is dropped.

So the SPIRIT of your concern is real but the MECHANISM is the opposite of what you thought: it's not a hardcoded allowlist of 3 models — it tiers everything. THE ACTUAL GAP is that classifier.py's regex table is itself a hand-maintained static list. A brand-new capable model with an unfamiliar slug silently lands in tier 2 until someone edits the file. That's the real stale-heuristic problem.

Where your instinct is EXACTLY right: ruflo's own ADR-149 (per-model cost-optimal routing) says the same thing about ruflo's 3-tier Haiku/Sonnet/Opus router — it calls the tier abstraction a "ceiling" and shows measured data where Llama-3.3-70B delivers 91% of Sonnet's quality at 0.9% of the cost, and GPT-4.1 outscores Sonnet at 4× less cost. ADR-149's whole thesis: drop the 3-tier rings, operate on concrete ModelId strings, pick the cheapest model predicted to clear a qualityBar. That is precisely the "not hardcoded rings/heuristics, leverage the dynamic list" approach you've been pushing. So you and ruflo's architects agree — and llm-gate's classifier.py is still stuck in the ring model you're rightly suspicious of.

---

OPENROUTER SUBAGENT TOOL — directly relevant to "assign any model to any subagent":
- The openrouter:subagent server tool lets a model delegate a sub-task to ANY OpenRouter model as a worker. Worker resolution precedence: parameters.model on the tool def → outer request model. "The delegating model does not choose its worker per call — the worker is fixed by the tool definition." So you pick the worker per-tool, not per-call. Any model, optionally with its own tools (web_search etc.). Each task independent, no parent context. This is the cleanest expression of "any model to any subagent" — and it's server-side, not our code.
- OpenRouter also has model "aliases" like ~openai/gpt-latest (always-resolves-latest) and a GET /api/v1/models full-catalog endpoint. So the dynamic list is fully available to us.

---

VOCALIZED IMPROVEMENT OPPORTUNITIES (speaking up, as you asked):

1. llm-gate classifier.py is the real "hardcoded heuristic" problem you sensed — just in a different file than you thought. RECOMMEND: replace the static regex tier table with either (a) OmniRoute's `auto`/`auto/coding`/`auto/cheap` server-side selection (it already scores health/quota/cost/speed/quality across ALL connected providers — exactly the dynamic list you want), or (b) adopt ruflo's ADR-149 pattern: per-ModelId cost-optimal selection with a qualityBar, fed by measured seed-corpus scores instead of hand-coded regexes. This collapses #57's "eligibility filtering" and the stale-classifier problem into one principled fix. The OmniRoute catalog + the existing ProbeRunner (already built, just unwired) give us the live health signal ADR-149 needs.

2. llm-gate and ruflo are solving the SAME routing problem twice with different abstractions (llm-gate = regex tiers; ruflo = bandit+tier; OmniRoute = 12-factor score). We should pick ONE authority. OmniRoute is already the live gateway and already does dynamic per-request selection with self-healing/circuit-breakers. llm-gate's value-add should be the CAPABILITY/CRITICALITY gating + probe truth (#57/#9), NOT re-implementing model scoring. Let OmniRoute own "which concrete model," llm-gate own "is it eligible + what criticality floor."

3. Codex workers: the runbook already documents the fix you hit — Codex CLI 0.144.6 regressed the custom-provider worker path; 0.144.5 is the proven pin, and omniroute launch-codex is the worker substrate. So "Codex workers aren't available" is a version-pin issue, not a dead end. We can fix it by pinning. I should NOT have been trying ruflo agent spawn --provider openrouter — that was the wrong harness and a hardcoded provider. The correct worker path is Codex-via-OmniRoute at the pinned version, OR Hermes delegate_task (which can take any model per call — that's our "any model to any subagent" already working).

4. Hermes model-catalog is CURATED/snapshot-based (maintainer-published JSON manifest), not a live full catalog. That's fine for a picker but means Hermes can't see newly-added models without a manifest republish. llm-gate, by contrast, tiers the live catalog. These two should reconcile — ideally both pull the OmniRoute /v1/models list as the single source of truth, with the manifest as a fallback only.

5. RuVector SONA / ReasoningBank / neural_train are already documented and wired (intelligence-route skill, hooks_model-* tools). The documented pattern for "learn from outcomes and route better" is: hooks_model-route → spawn at tier → hooks_model-outcome(success) → router learns. llm-gate has NONE of this feedback loop. If we want llm-gate's routing to improve over time instead of being a static regex, we should reuse ruflo's intelligence hooks (via the documented MCP/CLI surface) rather than reinventing a learner. That's the "use what we already have" point applied concretely.

6. OmniRoute's AUTO-COMBO already does bandit-style exploration (5% default, 10% on auto/smart) and self-healing (score<0.2 → excluded 5 min; >50% down → incident mode). That's the resilience layer llm-gate's probe idea is trying to rebuild. Reuse it; don't duplicate it.

---

WHERE I'M HONESTLY BLOCKED / INCOMPLETE (so you can decide):
- OpenRouter sub-page extraction 404'd for several routing pages (model-fallbacks, provider-selection, custom-classifiers, response-caching) — their URL scheme differs from the nav tree and my web_search is 401ing (Tavily creds expired), so I could not pull those specific pages. I DID get quickstart + subagent + the full llms.txt index. If you want those exact pages, I can clone the OpenRouter docs repo (if it's public) or you paste them.
- I have NOT read every single file in ruflo's v3/ tree (it's enormous — 314 MCP tools, 26 CLI commands, dozens of plugins). I read the load-bearing routing/intelligence/neural/DAA/SONA docs + the ADR that answers your question. If you want exhaustive v3 coverage I'll continue, but the design-pattern + dynamic-routing + neural questions are answered.

Next concrete step I'd take if you say go: open a GitHub issue (or PR) against llm-gate to replace classifier.py's static regex tiers with OmniRoute `auto`/per-ModelId cost-optimal selection (folding in the already-built ProbeRunner for live eligibility), citing ADR-149 as the proven pattern. That single change addresses your "dynamic list, not hardcoded rings" directive directly. Want me to draft that spec/issue?

## 🤖 Assistant (1784436320.4671986)

The AC is now crystal clear. The #57 contract is:

1. **Filter before ranking in every route path** (router/dispatcher/Gate/proxy).
2. **No ranker/Ruflo/RuVector can reintroduce excluded candidates** (invariant + tests).
3. **Explain shows complete pre-ranking eligible set + exclusions.** (#73)
4. **Protected work fails closed when runtime truth absent.**
5. **Integration tests for all public route entry points.**

#72 fills the gap: consult `AvailabilityCache.get(model_id)` inside Gate/dispatcher/router before ranking, drop `unknown`/`error` cached candidates for protected work.

Key architectural point from the docs (ENFORCEMENT_AND_LEARNING + ROUTING_POLICY): llm-gate must NOT couple to Ruflo/RuVector internals — and the probe should be OPT-IN (availability_profile=production). The existing `OmniRouteAvailabilityAdapter.evaluate()` already builds candidates; the missing piece is (a) probe truth feeding it, and (b) a single eligibility gate consumed by router/dispatcher/Gate/explain.

Let me read the remaining pieces I need: the rest of probes.py (openai_probe_transport + the classification `availability_state` mapping), intelligence.py route path, dispatcher.py, gate.py, and api.py explain endpoint. Then I'll design a single `EligibilityGate` and wire it.

```
17|@dataclass
18|class ReadinessReport:

20|    production_ready: bool
21|    profile: str
22|    managed_backend_status: str
23|    degraded_mode: bool
24|    policy_version: str
25|    reason: str

28|
29|class IntelligenceService:
30|    def __init__(
31|        self,
32|        primary_model: str,
33|        providers: dict[str, ProviderConfig],
34|        profile: str,
35|        log_path: str,
36|        log_full_task: bool,
37|        discovery_ttl: int,
38|        ruflo_command: str = \"ruflo\",
39|        ruvector_command: str = \"ruvector\",
40|        timeout_ms: int = 1000,
41|        frontier_allowlist: tuple[str, ...] | None = None,
42|        allow_client_model_override: bool = False,
43|        planner: StructuredPlanner | None = None,
44|    ):
45|        self.primary_model = primary_model
46|        self.providers = providers
47|        self.profile = profile
48|        self.log_path = log_path
49|        self.log_full_task = log_full_task
50|        self.discovery_ttl = discovery_ttl
51|        self.ruflo_command = ruflo_command
52|        self.ruvector_command = ruvector_command
53|        self.timeout_ms = timeout_ms
54|        self.frontier_allowlist = frontier_allowlist
55|        self.allow_client_model_override = allow_client_model_override
56|        self.planner = planner or StructuredPlanner()
57|        self.managed_backend_status = self._probe_managed_backend()
58|        self._policy_version = \"policy-2026-07-13.1\"
59|
60|    def _redact(self, text: str) -> str:
61|        import re
62|
63|        # Basic redaction before CLI execution (sk-...)
64|        return re.sub(r\"sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{10,}\", \"[REDACTED]\", text)
65|
66|    def _probe_managed_backend(self) -> str:
67|        try:
68|            import subprocess
69|
70|            result = subprocess.run(
71|                [self.ruflo_command, \"guidance\", \"gates\", \"--version\"],
72|                capture_output=True,
73|                text=True,
74|                timeout=0.5,
75|            )
76|            return \"healthy\" if result.returncode == 0 else \"unavailable\"
77|        except Exception:
78|            return \"unavailable\"
79|
80|    def readiness(self) -> ReadinessReport:
81|        status = (
82|            \"ready\"
83|            if self.profile != \"production\" or self.managed_backend_status != \"unavailable\"
84|            else \"not_ready\"
85|        )
86|        degraded = self.managed_backend_status == \"unavailable\"
87|        return ReadinessReport(
88|            status=status,
89|            production_ready=(not degraded),
90|            profile=self.profile,
91|            managed_backend_status=self.managed_backend_status,
92|            degraded_mode=degraded,
93|            policy_version=self._policy_version,
94|            reason=\"ready\" if not degraded else \"managed intelligence unavailable\",
95|            adapter_versions={},
96|        )
97|
98|    def route(
99|        self, task: str, criticality: str = \"medium\", context: dict[str, Any] | None = None
100|    ) -> RoutingDecision:
101|        start_t = time.time()
102|
103|        # Hard deterministic floor logic here.
104|        redacted_task = self._redact(task)
105|        # Attempt an async call or subprocess with timeout to Ruflo
106|        try:
107|            import subprocess
108|
109|            subprocess.run(
110|                [self.ruflo_command, \"hooks\", \"model-route\", \"--context\", redacted_task],
111|                capture_output=True,
112|                timeout=0.2,
113|            )
114|        except Exception:
115|            pass
116|
117|        # Fallback to strict heuristic scan
118|        eff_tier, heuristic_reason = scan(task)
119|
120|        # Planning estimates task capability needs. Criticality is retained as a
121|        # safety floor, not as a model selector: identical task semantics have
122|        # identical selection requirements unless a protected floor applies.
123|        try:
124|            task_spec = self.planner.plan(task, context=context, criticality=criticality).task_spec
125|            task_tier = {\"low\": 3, \"medium\": 2, \"high\": 1}.get(task_spec.effort, 2)
126|        except Exception:
127|            task_tier = 2
128|
129|        # Convert criticality string to required tier max
130|        tier_map = {\"critical\": 0, \"high\": 1, \"medium\": 2, \"low\": 3}
131|        req_tier = tier_map.get(criticality.lower(), 2)
132|
133|        esc_reason: str = \"\"
134|        escalated = False
135|
136|        if eff_tier is not None and eff_tier < req_tier:
137|            req_tier = eff_tier
138|            esc_reason = heuristic_reason or \"\"
139|            escalated = True
140|
141|        safety_floor = req_tier if req_tier <= 1 else 3
142|        final_tier = min(task_tier, safety_floor, eff_tier if eff_tier is not None else 3)
143|
144|        candidates = []
145|        for name, cfg in self.providers.items():
146|            candidates.extend(fetch_models(name, cfg, self.discovery_ttl))
147|
148|        best_model, _ = select_best_model(candidates, final_tier, self.providers)
149|
150|        if final_tier == 0 or not best_model:
151|            dec = RoutingDecision(
152|                model=self.primary_model,
153|                provider=\"primary\",
154|                tier=0,
155|                reason=\"critical — never offload\"
156|                if final_tier == 0
157|
```

```
{"content": "1|\"\"\"Bounded, availability-aware swarm assignment.
2|
3|This module is deliberately a planning contract: it never invokes a provider.  A
4|caller may use the returned assignment with a separately verified executor.
5|\"\"\"
6|
7|from __future__ import annotations
8|
9|from dataclasses import dataclass
10|from datetime import datetime, timezone
11|from typing import Any, Protocol
12|
13|from llm_gate.contracts import AvailabilitySnapshot, RuntimeCandidate
14|
15|
16|class SubagentExecutor(Protocol):
17|    \"\"\"Optional execution seam; live provider integrations are out of scope.\"\"\"
18|
19|    def execute(self, candidate: RuntimeCandidate, timeout_seconds: float) -> Any: ...
20|
21|
22|@dataclass(frozen=True)
23|class DispatchPolicy:
24|    \"\"\"Hard bounds and the narrow conditions under which escalation is legal.\"\"\"
25|
26|    required_capabilities: frozenset[str] = frozenset()
27|    verification_required: bool = False
28|    verification_capability: str = \"verification\"
29|    allow_escalation: bool = True
30|    max_escalation_depth: int = 1
31|    max_budget: float | None = None
32|    max_concurrency: int = 1
33|    timeout_seconds: float = 30.0
34|
35|
36|@dataclass(frozen=True)
37|class AssignmentExplanation:
38|    runtime_id: str
39|    selected: bool
40|    cost: float | None
41|    escalation_depth: int
42|    reasons: tuple[str, ...] = ()
43|
44|    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
45|        return {
46|            \"runtime_id\": self.runtime_id,
47|            \"selected\": self.selected,
48|            \"cost\": self.cost,
49|            \"escalation_depth\": self.escalation_depth,
50|            \"reasons\": list(self.reasons),
51|        }
52|
53|
54|@dataclass(frozen=True)
55|class DispatchResult:
56|    selected: RuntimeCandidate | None
57|    explanations: tuple[AssignmentExplanation, ...]
58|    eligible: tuple[RuntimeCandidate, ...] = ()
59|    dry_run: bool = True
60|    reason: str = \"\"
61|    estimated_cost: float = 0.0
62|    escalation_depth: int = 0
63|
64|    @property
65|    def assignment(self) -> RuntimeCandidate | None:
66|        return self.selected
67|
68|    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
69|        return {
70|            \"selected\": self.selected.to_dict() if self.selected else None,
71|            \"explanations\": [item.to_dict() for item in self.explanations],
72|            \"eligible\": [item.to_dict() for item in self.eligible],
73|            \"dry_run\": self.dry_run,
74|            \"reason\": self.reason,
75|            \"estimated_cost\": self.estimated_cost,
76|            \"escalation_depth\": self.escalation_depth,
77|        }
78|
79|
80|def _as_candidate(value: RuntimeCandidate | dict[str, Any]) -> RuntimeCandidate:
81|    return value if isinstance(value, RuntimeCandidate) else RuntimeCandidate.from_dict(value)
82|
83|
84|def _as_snapshot(value: AvailabilitySnapshot | dict[str, Any]) -> AvailabilitySnapshot:
85|    if isinstance(value, AvailabilitySnapshot):
86|        return value
87|    candidates = [_as_candidate(item) for item in value.get(\"candidates\", [])]
88|    return AvailabilitySnapshot.from_dict({**value, \"candidates\": candidates})
89|
90|
91|def _timestamp(value: str | None) -> datetime | None:
92|    if not value:
93|        return None
94|    try:
95|        return datetime.fromisoformat(value.replace(\"Z\", \"+00:00\")).astimezone(timezone.utc)
96|    except ValueError:
97|        return None
98|
99|
100|def _signal(candidate: RuntimeCandidate, *names: str) -> Any:
101|    lowered = {name.lower() for name in names}
102|    for key, value in candidate.signals.items():
103|        if key.lower() in lowered:
104|            return value
105|        if isinstance(value, dict) and any(k.lower() in lowered for k in value):
106|            return value
107|    return None
108|
109|
110|def _number(candidate: RuntimeCandidate, *names: str) -> float | None:
111|    value = _signal(candidate, *names)
112|    if isinstance(value, dict):
113|        matching = {n.lower() for n in names}
114|        value = next((v for k, v in value.items() if k.lower() in matching), value)
115|        if isinstance(value, dict):
116|            value = value.get(\"value\", value.get(\"amount\"))
117|    try:
118|        return None if value is None or isinstance(value, bool) else float(value)
119|    except (TypeError, ValueError):
120|        return None
121|
122|
123|def _cost(candidate: RuntimeCandidate) -> float | None:
124|    return _number(candidate, \"cost\", \"cost_usd\", \"estimated_cost\", \"usd\")
125|
126|
127|def _capabilities(candidate: RuntimeCandidate) -> frozenset[str]:
128|    return frozenset(item.lower() for item in candidate.capabilities)
129|
130|
131|class SwarmDispatcher:
132|    \"\"\"Select the least-cost candidate using only a caller-supplied snapshot.\"\"\"
133|
134|    def __init__(self, policy: DispatchPolicy | None = None, *, clock: Any = None) -> None:
135|        self.policy = policy or DispatchPolicy()
136|        self.clock = clock
137|
138|    def dispatch(
139|        self,
140|        snapshot: AvailabilitySnapshot | dict[str, Any],
141|        *,
142|        policy: DispatchPolicy | None = None,
143|        dry_run: bool = True,
144|        now: datetime | None = None,
145|    ) -> DispatchResult:
146|        active = policy or self.policy
147|        snap = _as_snapshot(snapshot)
148|        current = now or (self.clock() if self.clock else datetime.now(timezone.utc))
149|        if current.tzinfo is None:
150|            current = current.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
151|        candidates = tuple(_as_candidate(item) for item in snap.candidates)
152|        explanations: list[AssignmentExplanation] = []
153|        global_reason = self._snapshot_reason(snap, current)
154|        eligible: list[RuntimeCandidate] = []
155|        for candidate in candidates:
156|            reasons = list(global_reason or ())
157|            reasons.extend(self._reject_reasons(c
```

I have the full picture now. The architecture already has a clean separation:

- `OmniRouteAvailabilityAdapter.evaluate()` → `AvailabilityReport(tuple[AvailabilityCandidate...], tuple[eligible...], ...)` — the normalized eligibility source.
- `ProbeRunner.run()` exists & tested, returns `ProbeObservation` → `as_runtime_observation()` → `RuntimeObservation`.
- `SwarmDispatcher.dispatch()` already filters `eligible` by `live_eligible`/availability and records per-candidate exclusion reasons.
- `AvailabilityCache` (from #56) wraps `Callable[[], AvailabilityReport]`.
- `intelligence.route()` and `router.select_best_model()` do NOT currently consult availability at all.

The cleanest, doc-compliant #57 wiring (and #72/#73):

1. Add a **probe-enriched adapter variant** that runs the catalog/runtime evaluate AND a bounded `ProbeRunner` (only when `availability_profile` is production), merging probe `RuntimeObservation`s so `ready`/`degraded`/`denied` truth is applied. Reuse `ProbeRunner`, no reinvention.
2. Make **`AvailabilityCache` the single source of truth** and have router/dispatcher/Gate/explain all consult it (so no ranker can reintroduce excluded candidates — the invariant).
3. Extend **`/v1/route/explain` (#73)** to surface the full candidate set, eligible set, per-candidate exclusion reasons, and cache confidence/refresh_error — from the same gate.
4. **Fail-closed for protected work** when runtime truth absent.
